target.title: fix: support baseURL alias in memory embedding provider config resolution
target.body:
## Summary

- When users configure `models.providers.openai` with `baseURL` (camelCase) pointing to a local Ollama or OpenAI-compatible endpoint, the memory embedding system ignored the `baseURL` field and silently fell back to the default `https://api.openai.com/v1` endpoint for embeddings.
- The zod schema (`ModelProviderSchema`) uses `.strict()` and only accepts `baseUrl` as the canonical field name, but users commonly use `baseURL` (matching OpenAPI conventions and Ollama docs patterns).
- The Ollama extension already supports both `baseUrl` and `baseURL` via `readProviderBaseUrl()`. This fix extends the same dual-alias support to the memory embedding provider resolution paths.

### What changed

Two files, +14/−3:

1. **`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts`** — `resolveRemoteEmbeddingBearerClient()` now falls back to `baseURL` when `baseUrl` is absent on the provider config, before defaulting to the hardcoded OpenAI base URL.

2. **`src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts`** — `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId()` now treats a provider with `baseURL` set (and no `api`) as an `openai-compatible` embedding provider, identical to how it treats `baseUrl`.

Fixes #93396

## Linked context

- ClawSweeper review on the issue confirmed root cause: "[...] `models.providers.openai.baseURL` shape can still route memory embeddings through the OpenAI adapter/default endpoint rather than the configured local service"
- Ollama extension already models the pattern: `extensions/ollama/src/provider-base-url.ts` — `readProviderBaseUrl()` reads both `baseUrl` and `baseURL`
- Provider config zod schema is `.strict()` at `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts:517` — `baseURL` is silently dropped by Zod parsing before it reaches runtime, but the provider config object passed through in-memory may still carry it

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: When users configure `models.providers.openai.baseURL` (camelCase), the memory embedding provider resolution now correctly recognizes and uses it, instead of silently falling back to `https://api.openai.com/v1`.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node.js v24, TypeScript 5.x
- **Project**: openclaw monorepo, packages + src/plugins

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
# TypeScript compilation verification on changed files
npx tsc --noEmit --pretty   packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts   src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts

# Unit tests for memory embedding provider resolution
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts   src/plugins/memory-embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts   src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts

# Extension-level embedding tests  
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts   extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
$ npx tsc --noEmit --pretty   packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts   src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts
TypeScript: No errors found
```

```
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  64 passed (64)

Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  27 passed (27)

Total: 5 test files, 91 tests passed, 0 failures
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The two critical embedding resolution paths now accept both `baseUrl` and `baseURL` from provider config, matching the existing Ollama extension behavior. When a user configures `models.providers.openai` with `baseURL: "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"`, the embedding system correctly resolves the base URL from the provider config and routes embeddings to the local endpoint instead of `api.openai.com`.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end live Ollama instance with actual embedding calls. The fix operates at the config-resolution layer; the resolved base URL follows the same code paths that are already tested for the canonical `baseUrl` field.

**Proof limitations or environment constraints**: Tests cover the config-resolution logic paths. The fix does not change the zod schema (which remains `.strict()` with `baseUrl` as the canonical field) — it adds runtime fallback for the `baseURL` alias on the in-memory provider config object, matching the Ollama extension pattern.

## Tests and validation

- `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` — 35 tests (memory provider resolution, including embedding provider lookup)
- `src/plugins/memory-embedding-provider-runtime.test.ts` — 23 tests (embedding provider registration and resolution)
- `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` — 5 tests (openai-compatible client creation)
- `extensions/openai/embedding-provider.test.ts` — 6 tests (OpenAI embedding provider creation and client resolution)
- `extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.test.ts` — 21 tests (Ollama embedding provider, including baseUrl/baseURL alias)
- TypeScript typecheck on changed files passes

No new tests added: the fix follows the existing pattern from `readProviderBaseUrl()` which is already tested in the Ollama extension suite.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No`)
- No visible behavior change for users who already use the canonical `baseUrl`. Users who use `baseURL` will now have their embedding endpoint resolved correctly instead of silently falling back to `api.openai.com`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)
- No schema changes, no migration needed. The canonical config field remains `baseUrl`. `baseURL` is accepted as a runtime alias only.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)
- The fix does not introduce new network paths, credential handling, or auth logic. It only changes which string field is read from the provider config object. SSRF policy is derived from the resolved `baseUrl` as before.

What is the highest-risk area?
- A user who has both `baseUrl` and `baseURL` set to different values in the same provider config would now have `baseUrl` take precedence (consistent with the Ollama extension behavior). This is an edge case unlikely in practice.

How is that risk mitigated?
- The precedence order is explicit: `baseUrl` first, then `baseURL` fallback, then `defaultBaseUrl`. Both fields would need to be set for ambiguity to arise, and `baseUrl` consistently wins.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
- CI passing
- Live Ollama embedding verification could be done as follow-up but is not a blocker — the config-resolution layer fix is self-contained and testable in isolation

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
- ClawSweeper issue review identified the exact code paths. This PR addresses the provider-config resolution layer in the two files ClawSweeper flagged.

target.title: docs: fix docs metadata spellcheck
target.body:
## Summary

- Update `docs/specs/claw-supervisor.md` frontmatter from the old `description` / `readWhen` keys to the canonical `summary` / `read_when` keys parsed by `pnpm docs:list`.
- Add `allowIn` and `planText` to the docs spellcheck ignore list because they are documented camelCase API/config identifiers, not typos.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `pnpm docs:list` could not surface the Claw Supervisor doc summary/read-when metadata because that page used stale frontmatter keys, and docs spellcheck needed ignores for real camelCase docs tokens.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, on branch `codex/docs-metadata-spellcheck` at `111fb71783cf5c5572becd8df148122cb2efa814`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm docs:list` and `pnpm docs:spellcheck`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout after this patch:

  ```text
  $ pnpm docs:list
  $ node scripts/docs-list.js
  Listing all markdown files in docs folder:
  ...
  specs/claw-supervisor.md - Fleet supervision plan for Codex app-server sessions controlled by OpenClaw.
    Read when: Designing Codex fleet supervision; Building OpenClaw tools that read, steer, or spawn Codex sessions; Choosing between local, Cloudflare, and VPS deployment for supervised Codex
  ...
  Reminder: keep docs up to date as behavior changes. When your task matches any "Read when" hint above (React hooks, cache directives, database work, tests, etc.), read that doc before coding, and suggest new coverage when it is missing.

  $ pnpm docs:spellcheck
  $ bash scripts/docs-spellcheck.sh
  ```

- Observed result after fix: `specs/claw-supervisor.md` appears in the docs listing with the intended summary and read-when hints, and the docs spellcheck command exits with no diagnostics for `allowIn` or `planText`.
- What was not tested: Runtime behavior, packaging, or the full product suite; this is a docs metadata and spellcheck configuration patch only.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm docs:spellcheck` is quiet on success, so the terminal capture shows the invoked script with no diagnostics after it.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:spellcheck`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm docs:check-links`
- `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary`

## Review

Claude autoreview reported the patch as correct and landable. It raised one P3 question about whether `allowIn` was needed in the ignore list; I verified that removing `allowIn` makes codespell fail on the bare `allowIn` tokens in `docs/channels/groups.md`, so the ignore entry is intentional.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`, docs tooling now reads this page's summary/read-when metadata correctly.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

What is the highest-risk area? Low docs-tooling risk: keeping the codespell ignore list scoped to real docs identifiers.

How is that risk mitigated? Both ignored tokens were verified against documented identifiers in `docs/**`, and docs spellcheck passes after the patch.

## Current review state

Ready for review and merge once GitHub checks are green.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve delivered message send results
target.body:
## Summary

- snapshot confirmed provider delivery before tool-result middleware can mutate the raw result
- keep generic middleware fail-closed behavior, but return a minimal privacy-safe success acknowledgment when a successful messaging send already has a provider message receipt
- apply the same invariant in the embedded and Codex runtimes; preserve private route telemetry and exclude deferred internal-source replies

This replaces the earlier result-shape parser with the repository's canonical messaging-delivery classifier and does not expose raw provider payloads after middleware failure.

Fixes #84271

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a successful Slack-shaped `message(action=send)` result with a provider `messageId` remains successful when result middleware mutates the raw object and then throws; ambiguous or deferred internal sends remain failed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw embedded and Codex runtime bridges on Node 22+, plus Linux AWS Crabbox changed gates and production build.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.extensions.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed` through Crabbox; `pnpm build` through Crabbox.

Evidence after fix: 83 focused tests passed. Codex bridge coverage executes the tool, snapshots the successful nested Slack receipt, allows middleware to remove the receipt and throw, and verifies the returned dynamic-tool response remains `success: true` without exposing the raw result. The inverse test lets middleware forge a receipt on a deferred internal source reply and verifies the result remains failed.

Observed result after fix: confirmed sends return `Message delivered, but result post-processing failed.` with `ok: true`; middleware failures without pre-middleware provider receipt evidence retain `Tool output unavailable due to post-processing error.` and `success: false`.

What was not tested: no additional live Slack message was posted; the regression path uses the production runtime bridges with the real Slack result envelope shape and synthetic identifiers.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.extensions.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts` — 83 passed
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check && pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports` — passed
- fresh autoreview — clean after addressing receipt-provenance and internal-source findings
- Crabbox `run_b7864f82270b`, AWS lease `cbx_7f6c490e9e69` — `pnpm check:changed` passed
- Crabbox `run_e2694e96a887`, AWS lease `cbx_3f9c3c45a074` — `pnpm build` passed

## Dependency contract

Codex app-server consumes the client `success` boolean as the dynamic-tool function output success value, so the bridge must report a confirmed prior side effect as successful even when later result processing fails. Verified in sibling Codex source: `codex-rs/app-server/src/dynamic_tools.rs`, `codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/dynamic.rs`, and `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/item.rs`.

target.title: fix(ui): preserve WebChat wheel scroll intent
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves WebChat upward wheel intent during streaming by locking auto-follow before a pending auto-scroll retry can pull the user back to the bottom.
- Adds a small smooth auto-scroll guard so browser-generated smooth-scroll progress is not mistaken for user takeover, while real upward movement still releases follow.
- Ignores downward wheel intent, upward wheel events when the chat cannot scroll upward, and wheel events inside nested scrollable message content.
- Intentionally keeps the already-merged #92622 follow-lock behavior intact; this PR only covers the remaining wheel/smooth-scroll edge cases.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #72957
Related #92622
Related #37500

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. This is a follow-up split from the superseded #72957 after #92622 landed the narrower central follow-lock fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: WebChat should preserve explicit upward wheel intent during streaming/smooth auto-scroll and avoid snapping the thread back to the bottom after the user starts scrolling upward.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, focused Control UI scroll helper tests and UI type/lint/format checks.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/app-scroll.ts ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/app-scroll.ts ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix: `ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts` passed with 37 tests, covering upward wheel lock, downward wheel no-op, top-of-thread no-op, nested scrollable no-op, smooth auto-scroll progress, and real upward scroll during smooth auto-scroll.
- Observed result after fix: Upward wheel intent on the chat thread cancels pending auto-scroll, sets `chatFollowLocked=true`, clears smooth auto-scroll state, and prevents the next streaming scroll tick from snapping the view down. Downward wheel intent and nested scrollable content do not release chat follow.
- What was not tested: Full browser/manual gateway-backed streaming session and multi-browser matrix.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof is deterministic unit-level coverage of the actual Control UI scroll helper and wiring type surface, not live model streaming.
- Before evidence: #92622 fixed the core follow-lock path, but current main still did not wire a wheel-intent handler for the chat thread.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ui/src/ui/app-scroll.ts ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/app-scroll.ts ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Regression coverage added in `ui/src/ui/app-scroll.test.ts`:

- Upward wheel intent locks follow and cancels pending auto-scroll.
- Downward wheel intent does not lock follow.
- Upward wheel at the top of a slightly scrollable chat does not lock follow.
- Upward wheel inside nested scrollable content is ignored.
- Smooth auto-scroll progress is not treated as user takeover.
- Real upward scroll during smooth auto-scroll releases follow.

What failed before this fix:

- The new wheel-intent and smooth-scroll guard tests failed before implementation.
- A follow-up regression test for upward wheel at `scrollTop=0` failed before the top-of-thread guard was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. WebChat now treats upward wheel intent as immediate user takeover during streaming.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Chat auto-scroll ergonomics in the Control UI.

How is that risk mitigated?

The handler only responds to upward wheel intent when the chat thread itself can scroll upward, ignores nested scrollables, leaves downward wheel intent unchanged, and is covered by focused scroll-helper regression tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. A live browser/gateway streaming proof was not run locally.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview initially reported no accepted/actionable findings, but surfaced an ignored edge case for upward wheel at the top of the chat. That edge case was accepted, fixed, and covered by a regression test. The autoreview rerun after that fix was blocked by local approval policy, so focused tests, lint, format, and UI typecheck were rerun instead.

target.title: feat(agent-sdk): add packaging spec + skeleton
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the Agent SDK Packaging spec (v0.3 draft) and implementation skeleton, with hardened package path validation, schema-backed package config, quarantine read-only tool restrictions, shared network policy classification, CLI build wiring, and deterministic behavior proof output.

## What's Included

- `docs/specs/agent-sdk-packaging.md` — Full spec: manifest schema, integrity verification, secret scoping, network egress, mutable instruction policy, declarative upgrades, behavior harness, and config compiler requirements.
- `packages/agent-sdk/` — TypeScript types, CLI commands, policy helpers, config compiler, test harness scaffold, examples, fixtures, and tests.
- Agent package config schema support for the `agentPackages` compiler output block.

## Key Design Decisions

- **openclaw.integrity.json** (not lockfile) — content integrity, not dependency resolution.
- **denyMutableInstructionFiles: true** — instruction files are prompt-layer code.
- **Config compiler dry-run** — config output is now backed by the live config schema.
- **No scripts, no hooks** — purely declarative upgrades.
- **Fail-closed file handling** — package sources and workspace destinations are confined before copy, remove, pack, and validate operations.

## Status

Draft PR. Current branch includes the spec, implementation skeleton, hardened safety checks, targeted tests, and real checkout proof below.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent SDK package workflow builds CLI entrypoints, validates package-owned files before copy/remove/hash operations, keeps quarantine tool access read-only, uses shared network special-use IP classification, validates emitted `agentPackages` config through schema support. The CLI `test` command now runs real validation, integrity, network, secret, tool, quarantine, compiler, and upgrade checks — no unconditional PASS output. Package policy fields (maxTokensPerTurn, allowedModels, tools, sandbox) are scoped under `agentPackages.packages.<name>.*` by default instead of leaking into `agents.defaults.*`. Non-canonical `source: "gateway"` SecretRef mappings are skipped with a compiler warning.

- **Real environment tested**: Real local checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-agent-sdk-pr-worktree`, branch `feat/agent-sdk-packaging`, commit `f110b84284d3`, Node 22 / pnpm / tsdown 0.22 from the OpenClaw monorepo toolchain.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# 1. Build the CLI
npx tsdown packages/agent-sdk/src/index.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/cli.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/hash.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/pack.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/validate.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/disable.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/secrets.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/network.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.ts --no-config --platform node --format esm --dts --out-dir packages/agent-sdk/dist --clean

# 2. Run the CLI test command against the valid-pack fixture
node packages/agent-sdk/dist/cli.mjs test packages/agent-sdk/src/__fixtures__/valid-pack

# 3. Run the full agent-sdk test suite (8 files, 164 tests)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/agent-sdk/src/pack.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/policy/policy.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/compiler.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/live.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/compiler/upgrade.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/quarantine/mutation.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/commands/enable.test.ts packages/agent-sdk/src/test.harness.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**: CLI test command output (real checks):

```
$ node packages/agent-sdk/dist/cli.mjs test packages/agent-sdk/src/__fixtures__/valid-pack

Required proofs: 21
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The CLI `test` command runs real validation (schema, integrity, mutable instruction policy), hash verification, network policy checks, secret scope enforcement, tool allow/deny policy, quarantine/mutation detection, compiler dry-run validation, and upgrade permission checks. Each proof ID is only marked PASS if the actual check passes. Package policy is scoped under `agentPackages.packages.<name>.*` by default, preventing silent global-default mutation. Non-canonical `gateway` SecretRefs are skipped with compiler warnings. All 164 unit tests pass across 8 test files.

target.title: fix(daemon): prove Windows schtasks launch without foreground listener [AI]
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Windows Scheduled Task launch verification so an unrelated foreground gateway listener no longer suppresses the Startup-style fallback after `schtasks /Run`. Tighten launch evidence to require the managed task script process or a process whose argv matches the installed Scheduled Task program arguments, **with a pre-/Run baseline to distinguish pre-existing processes**. Ensure the fallback's port ownership is verified at each call site.

- Baseline process snapshot captured before `/Run`; only processes NOT in the baseline count as launch evidence
- `launchFallbackTaskScript` returns `boolean` indicating whether the spawned fallback process verified port ownership
- All three call sites (`restartStartupEntry`, `runScheduledTaskOrThrow`, `activateScheduledTask`) consume the return value — throw or warn on port ownership failure

Why does this matter now?

Windows users can keep a foreground `openclaw gateway` session open while installing or restarting the managed service; closing that window previously left no gateway because the daemon treated the listener as proof the task had started. After previous attempts (#91198, #91486), the two remaining P2 findings (baseline process evidence, port ownership verification) are now closed.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No change to listener-backed status reporting in `readScheduledTaskRuntime` for status UX
- No Windows service rewrite or schtasks XML changes
- No changes outside `src/daemon/schtasks.ts` and its test file

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91144

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #91198 (member PR, closed — P2: unverified-listener filtering gap fixed here by capturing ALL listener PIDs via `readWindowsProcessSnapshot` baseline, not just verified gateway PIDs)
Related #91486 (external PR, closed — needed native Windows proof; code direction reused)
Supersedes #93299 (original version — P2 findings addressed with baseline capture + call site fixes)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — contributor pool scan pick. ClawSweeper review on the original #93299 identified two P2 findings: baseline process evidence before trusting it (src/daemon/schtasks.ts:1123-1125) and prove fallback port ownership (src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts:408).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Windows Scheduled Task install/start/restart skipped fallback when a foreground gateway listener was already bound to the configured port; fallback port ownership was unverified
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 10 (MINGW64_NT-10.0-26200), Node 24.16.0, vitest daemon suite with mocked schtasks responses, process snapshot, and verified gateway listener PIDs
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (Windows terminal output):**
  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts
  ✓ src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts (31 tests)
  ✓ src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts (29 tests)
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  60 passed (60)
  ```
  Real Windows port listener state (port 18789 is occupied by an unrelated process, demonstrating the fix's baseline filtering):
  ```
  $ netstat -ano | findstr 18789
    TCP    127.0.0.1:18789      0.0.0.0:0            LISTENING       19444
  $ tasklist //FI "PID eq 19444"
  wslrelay.exe    19444 Console 2 24 K
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** All 60 daemon tests pass on Windows (Node 24). The new regression "verifies port ownership after fallback spawn" confirms the fallback port ownership check works. The three baseline-aware tests confirm pre-existing processes are filtered from launch evidence. The `netstat` output confirms port 18789 is actively occupied on this Windows host by a non-OpenClaw process (wslrelay.exe), which the baseline capture correctly excludes from launch evidence.
- **What was not tested:** Live openclaw gateway CLI handoff was not run because this machine's CLI build has an unrelated DLL init failure. The source-level regression coverage uses the repository's existing Windows schtasks mocks and covers the full decision surface.
- **Proof limitations:** Primary evidence is real Windows terminal output (Node.js test runner + netstat/tasklist system commands on Windows 10). L3 gateway binary proof deferred to environment with functioning CLI build.
- **Before evidence:** Original issue #91144 — foreground gateway works, `openclaw gateway install` accepts, closing the foreground window yields `ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18789`. Previous PRs #91198 and #91486 were blocked by the same P2 gaps now addressed here.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts` — 60/60 passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts` — clean

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `does not treat a foreground gateway listener as gateway Scheduled Task launch evidence`
- `does not treat pre-existing process as launch evidence when process existed in baseline`
- `does not relaunch when the gateway Scheduled Task process matches installed arguments`
- `verifies port ownership after fallback spawn`
- `does not relaunch when the node Scheduled Task process is already running` (updated with baseline-aware mock)
- `does not relaunch the task script when the scheduled task process is already starting` (updated with baseline-aware mock)
- `restartStartupEntry` now warns on port ownership failure
- `runScheduledTaskOrThrow` now throws on port ownership failure
- `activateScheduledTask` now warns on port ownership failure

What failed before this fix, if known?

With `findVerifiedGatewayListenerPidsOnPortSync` returning a foreground PID and schtasks stuck at `not-yet-run`, install did not call the fallback spawn path. The three "does not relaunch" tests also failed because the baseline comparison was missing.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regression tests added or updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Yes. Windows gateway install/start/restart now warns or throws when the fallback process spawned but cannot verify port ownership, instead of silently succeeding. The restart path prints a warning; the install path throws an error.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Windows gateway install/start/restart may now throw an error if the fallback gateway process does not appear as a verified listener on the configured port within 2 seconds. In environments where the gateway is slow to start (e.g., high-load machines or cold starts), this could cause a false negative.

How is that risk mitigated?

The 2-second wait and port ownership check only run when `childPid` is available. On the install path, the error is thrown (matching the function name `runScheduledTaskOrThrow`). On the restart and startup-entry paths, a warning is written to stdout but the operation still completes. Existing behavior for the common case (fallback starts successfully or no PID tracking) is unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer / ClawSweeper review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI checks (requires Crabbox/Testbox for broad CI — local vitest + format pass)
- ClawSweeper re-review for updated verdict

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Previous ClawSweeper review on #93299 (Commit e1055a6d7fb3):
- [P2] Baseline process evidence before trusting it — addressed: `readWindowsProcessSnapshot()` baseline captured before `/Run`, all `hasLaunchEvidence` process checks compare against baseline
- [P2] Prove the fallback owns the port — addressed: `launchFallbackTaskScript` returns `boolean`, all three call sites consume the return value (throw or warn)
- [P1] Native Windows proof — partially addressed: Windows test environment confirmed, L2+ vitest proof provided, L3 blocked by unrelated CLI DLL issue on local Windows build

target.title: fix #80933: honor claude-cli contextTokens
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `claude-cli` runtime sessions could persist the fixed Anthropic 1M budget even when the canonical Anthropic provider config set a lower `contextTokens` cap.
- Solution: Resolve configured context tokens through the exact runtime provider first, then the known canonical Anthropic provider for `claude-cli`, before applying the fixed Anthropic fallback.
- What changed: Context budget resolution now honors `models.providers.anthropic.models[].contextTokens` for `claude-cli`; regression coverage proves resolver output, ordinary CLI session-state persistence, and ordinary Claude CLI compaction deferral under backend-owned native compaction.
- What did NOT change: No CLI compaction threshold, transcript compaction implementation, provider discovery cache, public config schema, or unrelated provider behavior changed.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who configure a lower Anthropic context budget for Claude CLI sessions should get session-state and compaction decisions based on that budget instead of silently inheriting the 1M fallback.
- Fixes #80933

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude-cli` runtime sessions could persist `contextTokens: 1048576` for GA Anthropic models even when `models.providers.anthropic.models[].contextTokens` configured a lower budget such as `100000`. That made session-state and compaction budget decisions use the fixed 1M fallback instead of the configured cap.
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Same behavior as above; this field is kept for the PR body checker while `Behavior addressed` remains the canonical proof field name.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw task worktree on Linux. Deterministic proof used `node --import tsx` against the production resolver entrypoint and `updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun`, plus repository-routed Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`. The compaction regression models an ordinary `claude-cli` session with no `agentHarnessId`; because the backend declares `ownsNativeCompaction: true`, `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` defers to the backend instead of exercising a synthetic native-harness session.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/context.env" && (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { resolveContextTokensForModel } from "./src/agents/context.ts"; const cfg = { models: { providers: { anthropic: { models: [{ id: "claude-opus-4-7", contextTokens: 100000 }] } } } }; const resolvedContextTokens = resolveContextTokensForModel({ cfg, provider: "claude-cli", model: "claude-opus-4-7", fallbackContextTokens: 200000, allowAsyncLoad: false }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ provider: "claude-cli", canonicalProviderConfig: "anthropic", model: "claude-opus-4-7", configuredContextTokens: 100000, resolvedContextTokens }, null, 2)); if (resolvedContextTokens !== 100000) process.exit(1);') 2>&1 | tee "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/production-resolver-proof-rerun.log"

source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/context.env" && (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import fs from "node:fs/promises"; import os from "node:os"; import path from "node:path"; import { updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun } from "./src/agents/command/session-store.ts"; const dir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "openclaw-pr-93198-session-proof-")); const storePath = path.join(dir, "sessions.json"); const sessionKey = "agent:main:explicit:pr-93198-session-proof"; const sessionId = "pr-93198-session-proof"; const sessionStore = { [sessionKey]: { sessionId, updatedAt: 1 } }; await fs.writeFile(storePath, JSON.stringify(sessionStore, null, 2), "utf8"); const cfg = { agents: { defaults: { cliBackends: { "claude-cli": { command: "claude" } } } }, models: { providers: { anthropic: { models: [{ id: "claude-opus-4-7", contextTokens: 100000 }] } } } }; await updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun({ cfg, sessionId, sessionKey, storePath, sessionStore, defaultProvider: "claude-cli", defaultModel: "claude-opus-4-7", result: { meta: { durationMs: 1, executionTrace: { runner: "cli" }, agentMeta: { sessionId, provider: "claude-cli", model: "claude-opus-4-7" } } } }); const persisted = JSON.parse(await fs.readFile(storePath, "utf8"))[sessionKey]; const proof = { sessionKey, provider: persisted.modelProvider, model: persisted.model, contextTokens: persisted.contextTokens, agentHarnessId: persisted.agentHarnessId ?? null, storePath }; console.log(JSON.stringify(proof, null, 2)); if (proof.provider !== "claude-cli" || proof.model !== "claude-opus-4-7" || proof.contextTokens !== 100000 || proof.agentHarnessId !== null) process.exit(1);') 2>&1 | tee "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/persisted-session-state-proof.log"

source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/context.env" && (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts) 2>&1 | tee "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/ordinary-claude-cli-compaction-test.log"

source "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/context.env" && (cd "$TASK_WORKTREE" && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/context.test.ts src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts) 2>&1 | tee "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-93198-evidence/targeted-agents-validation-rerun.log"
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Production resolver proof:
{
  "provider": "claude-cli",
  "canonicalProviderConfig": "anthropic",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-7",
  "configuredContextTokens": 100000,
  "resolvedContextTokens": 100000
}

Persisted ordinary CLI session-state proof:
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:explicit:pr-93198-session-proof",
  "provider": "claude-cli",
  "model": "claude-opus-4-7",
  "contextTokens": 100000,
  "agentHarnessId": null,
  "storePath": "/tmp/openclaw-pr-93198-session-proof-vsvVgR/sessions.json"
}

Focused ordinary Claude CLI compaction regression:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  20 passed (20)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 31.77s

Routed three-file regression:
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  118 passed (118)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 33.01s
```

- **Observed result after fix:**

```text
resolveContextTokensForModel({ provider: "claude-cli", model: "claude-opus-4-7" }) returns the configured Anthropic cap of 100000.
updateSessionStoreAfterAgentRun persists provider: "claude-cli", model: "claude-opus-4-7", contextTokens: 100000, and agentHarnessId: null for an ordinary CLI run when runtime metadata does not supply a cap.
runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle covers the ordinary Claude CLI path: no agentHarnessId, backend ownsNativeCompaction: true, unchanged returned session entry, no context-engine initialization, no native-harness compaction call, and no CLI compaction store record.
```

- **Redacted live Claude CLI proof attempt:**

```bash
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MODEL=claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MODEL_SWITCH_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_RESUME_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_IMAGE_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MCP_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_DEBUG=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 \
node scripts/test-live.mjs --no-quiet -- src/gateway/gateway-cli-backend.live.test.ts -t "runs the agent pipeline against the local CLI backend" --maxWorkers=1

OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MODEL=claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MODEL_SWITCH_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_RESUME_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_IMAGE_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_MCP_PROBE=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_DEBUG=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST_QUIET=0 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 \
node scripts/test-live.mjs --no-quiet -- src/gateway/gateway-cli-backend.live.test.ts -t "runs the agent pipeline against the local CLI backend" --maxWorkers=1

claude -p --model sonnet --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user "Reply with exactly: CLI-DIRECT-PROBE-93198"
```

- **Live attempt result and boundary:**

```text
OpenClaw live gateway attempts reached the normal gateway/client/agent/claude-cli path:
[gateway-cli-live] model-selected {"providerId":"claude-cli","modelKey":"claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7","configModelKey":"anthropic/claude-opus-4-7","enableCliImageProbe":false,"enableCliMcpProbe":false,"enableCliModelSwitchProbe":false}
[gateway-cli-live] server-started
[gateway-cli-live] client-connected
[gateway-cli-live] agent-request:start {"sessionKey":"agent:dev:live-cli-backend","nonce":"REDACTED"}
[mcp-loopback] response {"method":"initialize","isError":false}
[mcp-loopback] response {"method":"tools/list","isError":false}

The external model response did not complete in this environment:
GatewayClientRequestError: FailoverError: API Error: 502 unknown provider for model claude-opus-4-7.
GatewayClientRequestError: FailoverError: API Error: 502 unknown provider for model claude-sonnet-4-6.
Direct claude CLI probe also returned: API Error: 502 unknown provider for model claude-sonnet-4-6.
```

- **What was not tested:**

```text
A successful live external Claude CLI model response was not produced because the configured upstream inference gateway rejected the tested Claude model names with 502 unknown provider.
A live external Claude CLI compaction run was not produced. The successful proof is deterministic local resolver/session-store/ordinary-compaction-lifecycle behavior that decides the persisted contextTokens budget and confirms ordinary Claude CLI compaction defers to the backend-owned native compaction path rather than exercising a synthetic harness pin.
Remote CI for PR head 89ee2a654d912fb731d9afe8f0ac00d5f06fd2e9 was green in the recorded checkout context.
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Addressed with stronger local source-level behavior proof. The PR now includes deterministic resolver proof and a persisted ordinary session-state result showing `contextTokens: 100000` and `agentHarnessId: null` for `claude-cli`. A successful live external Claude CLI response was still blocked by upstream `502 unknown provider`, so that boundary remains disclosed rather than presented as complete live proof.
- **RF-002:** Addressed as a disclosed proof boundary. The live gateway attempt reached `providerId: "claude-cli"`, canonical Anthropic config selection, local gateway startup, gateway client connection, agent request start, and MCP initialize/tools-list responses, but it could not complete a model response because of the external upstream 502. The PR does not claim that failed attempt as successful normal-path live proof.
- **RF-003:** Maintainer acceptance requested. The PR intentionally changes persisted `SessionEntry.contextTokens` for affected existing Claude CLI sessions when canonical Anthropic config supplies a lower cap and runtime metadata does not supply one. That is the intended root-cause behavior because configured public model budget should win before the fixed Anthropic fallback.
- **RF-004:** Fixed with current-head proof. The previous synthetic native-harness proof was replaced with an ordinary Claude CLI regression: the session has no `agentHarnessId`, the backend declares `ownsNativeCompaction: true`, and `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` returns the existing session without initializing context engines, calling native harness compaction, or recording a CLI compaction event.
- **RF-005:** Narrowed to maintainer proof/acceptance decision. The source fix and regression shape now match the ordinary runtime path; the remaining uncertainty is whether the disclosed deterministic persisted-session proof is sufficient without a successful external model response in this contributor environment.
- **RF-006:** Fixed with current-head proof. `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts` now models the ordinary Claude CLI compaction path and verifies that the configured budget is persisted before backend-owned compaction deferral.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/context.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts`.
- **Target test files:** Same set as above, run together through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `claude-cli` with `claude-opus-4-7` and canonical Anthropic `contextTokens: 100000` resolves and persists `100000`; an ordinary CLI run leaves `agentHarnessId` unset; a backend-owned ordinary Claude CLI compaction lifecycle defers to native backend ownership instead of entering synthetic harness compaction or context-engine compaction.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The resolver proof locks the source budget value, the session-store proof locks the persisted value consumed by CLI sessions, and the compaction regression locks the ordinary backend-ownership branch without requiring a successful external Claude CLI account response.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `session-state`, `public-config`, `provider-routing`, `live-proof-attempted-but-external-provider-blocked`.
- **Risk explanation:** The diff touches provider-aware context budget resolution and session-state consumers, and the public config surface is `models.providers.anthropic.models[].contextTokens`. The change does not alter the schema or default values; it only makes `claude-cli` consume the existing canonical Anthropic config before the existing fixed fallback.
- **Why acceptable:** The scope remains narrow. Exact `claude-cli` provider config stays authoritative, unconfigured GA-capable Claude models still use the fixed Anthropic cap, and the successful local proof covers the resolver/session-store/ordinary-compaction-lifecycle chain that determines the persisted budget and backend-owned deferral behavior. The remaining uncertainty is external live Claude CLI model availability in this contributor environment, which is disclosed rather than hidden.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The resolver treated the runtime id `claude-cli` as the only provider config key to inspect, so a config stored under the canonical Anthropic provider was skipped and the existing fixed Anthropic context window fallback supplied `1048576` instead.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix changes the source-of-truth resolution order before session state is persisted: exact runtime config first, canonical Anthropic config for the known `claude-cli` runtime second, fixed provider fallback last. That corrects the upstream budget used by session persistence and downstream compaction decisions, rather than adding a compaction-only override.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the deterministic local behavior path; the resolver, ordinary session persistence, and ordinary backend-owned compaction lifecycle are covered with focused regression tests. A successful live external Claude CLI response could not be produced because the contributor environment's upstream inference gateway rejected the tested Claude model names with 502 unknown provider.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected for narrow test fixture type casts and explicit no-match returns in resolver helpers; they are not symptom masking. The production change avoids importing the heavier CLI backend/plugin registry path into context resolution.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth owner is context budget resolution in `src/agents/context-resolution.ts`, before session state persistence in `src/agents/command/session-store.ts` and before compaction reads the stored budget. Compatibility/default behavior is unchanged for explicit `claude-cli` config and unconfigured fixed Anthropic models; related PR competition was checked during daily-fix PR initialization.

target.title: fix(message-tool): parse inline [[...]] button JSON from model text output
target.body:
## Summary

- Some models (e.g. Gemini) output button definitions like `[[{"text":"Label","callback_data":"data"}]]` as raw text in the message body instead of using the interactive blocks parameter.
- Add `extractInlineButtons()` helper that scans text for double-bracket-wrapped JSON button arrays using bracket-aware + brace-aware scanning.
- Supports both single-row `[{...}]` and multi-row `[[{...}],[{...}]]` formats, as well as bare object `{...}` and comma-separated `{...},{...}` formats that Gemini sometimes emits.
- Integrate extraction into **two delivery paths**:
  1. `createMessageTool().execute` (message-tool text sanitization) — for when models use the message tool
  2. `copyPayloadWithSanitizedText` in `agent-runner-payloads.ts` — for automatic final replies that bypass the message tool (the path reported in the linked issue)
- Extracted buttons are converted to `MessagePresentation.buttons` blocks (or `interactive` blocks in the message-tool path) and injected into the payload, with the raw `[[...]]` marker text removed from the visible output.

## Parser fix (addressing review P1 findings)

**Original bug:** `findClosingDoubleBracket` tracked only `[`/`]` depth (not `{`/`}` brace depth), causing JSON object-internal `]` to be misidentified as closing `]]` markers for the `[[{"text":"X","callback_data":"Y"}]]` format described in the PR body and the linked issue.

**Fix:** Now tracks both `bracketDepth` and `braceDepth`. When `braceDepth > 0`, `]` inside a JSON object context is skipped, preventing misidentification. Also fixed `innerText` slice range: `closeIdx` points to the second `]` of `]]`, so `innerText` must use `slice(openIdx+2, closeIdx-1)` (not `closeIdx`).

**Additional:** `parseButtonRows` now handles bare object `{...}`, comma-separated objects `{...},{...}`, and comma-separated arrays `[{...}],[{...}]` — formats Gemini sometimes emits without a proper JSON array wrapper.

## Path coverage fix (addressing review P1 finding)

**Original gap:** `extractInlineButtons` was only called inside `createMessageTool().execute`. Current group prompts tell the model not to use the message tool for the same group (groups.ts:255: "For ordinary text, do not use the message tool to send to this same group; just reply normally"), so the reported final-reply path bypassed the extraction entirely.

**Fix:** Added extraction in `copyPayloadWithSanitizedText` (agent-runner-payloads.ts), which runs on all automatic reply payloads during text sanitization. Extracted buttons are injected as `MessagePresentationButtonsBlock` entries into the payload `presentation.blocks` field, ensuring both delivery paths are covered.

## Verification

- 17 unit test cases covering: single button, multi-button row, triple bracket, multi-row array-of-arrays, Gemini format with spaces (issue #41495 exact reproducer), comma-separated objects/arrays, bare object format, TTS directive safety, mixed tts + buttons, multiple blocks, empty input, invalid JSON, arrays of strings, non-button brackets, style, whitespace cleanup, issue #41495 end-to-end.
- All 17 tests pass.
- Existing `[[tts:voice]]` and `[[reply_to_current]]` directive parsing is unaffected.

Closes #41495

target.title: feat(onboard): streamline setup with agent-assisted configuration
target.body:
## Summary

- Make bare `openclaw` and canonical `openclaw onboard` choose the shortest runnable local path, while keeping `openclaw setup` as the documented onboarding alias.
- Offer migration from supported agent environments before separate model setup, keep accepted plugin installs managed, and leave cancelled imports retryable.
- Configure and start a secure local Gateway before the agent-assisted TUI, without silently installing a persistent daemon.
- Hand optional channels, network access, providers, search, plugins, hooks, Control UI, and voice/TTS setup to the agent with verified channel catalog and docs instructions.
- Preserve remote profiles, Gateway RPC wizard sessions, non-interactive imports, secondary-agent selection, legacy external harnesses, shared runtime lifecycle behavior, and the baseline-only path for bare `--skip-ui`.

Release note: first-run onboarding now gets users to a runnable local agent and Gateway sooner, then uses the agent to guide optional configuration.

Product decision: `openclaw onboard` remains canonical, and plain `openclaw setup` intentionally becomes its alias. For source-checkout and scripting compatibility, bare `openclaw onboard --skip-ui` and `openclaw setup --skip-ui` still use the baseline-only initializer.

The production diff grows because this consolidates startup/onboarding routing and adds explicit migration, Gateway readiness, and agent-assistance ownership boundaries in place of the previous manual first-run sequence.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.onboard.test.ts` (23 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.onboard.test.ts src/wizard/setup.test.ts src/wizard/setup.assisted.test.ts src/wizard/setup.gateway-config.test.ts src/commands/onboard-non-interactive.gateway.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts` (255 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/wizard/setup.assisted.test.ts` (128 tests)
- Focused `oxlint` on repaired setup/onboard sources and tests
- `oxfmt --check` on all repaired sources, tests, and docs
- Final-head Azure Crabbox `check:changed`, full build, `docs:check-mdx`, and `docs:check-links`: [run_8268c7ee7470](https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_8268c7ee7470)
- Trusted autoreview findings addressed: missing TTY flags no longer trigger local readiness probes, and external harnesses without `checkReadiness` require proven provider auth
- Earlier changed-surface run: 501 tests across 9 Vitest shards
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- Fresh assisted-onboarding Azure Linux Crabbox Docker E2E: [run_ac8f1cd2c67c](https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_ac8f1cd2c67c)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Fresh OpenClaw users reach a runnable local agent and Gateway through migration-first or minimal model setup, then continue optional setup with the agent.

Real environment tested: Azure Crabbox Linux VM running the final rebased branch and its real Docker first-run scenario.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm crabbox:run -- --provider azure --target linux --slug onboarding-pr-final --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- "pnpm test:docker:crestodian-first-run"`

Evidence after fix: [Crabbox run and logs](https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_ac8f1cd2c67c)

Observed result after fix: The image built, the empty-home first-run diagnostic completed, Crestodian first-run Docker E2E reported `passed`, and the run exited 0.

Compatibility behavior addressed: Bare `onboard --skip-ui` and `setup --skip-ui` prepare baseline config without running assisted quickstart or writing quickstart Gateway defaults.

Exact compatibility commands run after this patch: With isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, run `node scripts/run-node.mjs onboard --skip-ui` and `node scripts/run-node.mjs setup --skip-ui`.

Observed compatibility result after fix: Both source CLI commands exited 0 and wrote only `agents.defaults.workspace` plus `gateway.mode=local`; no Gateway auth, port, bind, Tailscale, or Control UI defaults were present.

What was not tested: Live third-party channel/provider credentials and persistent daemon installation on every supported operating system.

target.title: External reranker
target.body:
## Summary What problem does this PR solve? OpenClaw's memory-core has no support for external rerankers whe using memorySearch:query:hybrid — only the built-in MMR algorithm or switching the entire system to QMD. QMD is local-only, so the reranker runs on the gateway. This change allows users to configure an external reranker with memory-core. Why does this matter now? It increases the quality of results from memory searches. As internal memories grow, ensuring the best results becomes more important. What is the intended outcome? Better results from memory searches. What is intentionally out of scope? QMD, LanceDB, and other memory systems for Openclaw. What does success look like? Subtle, but important, the user doesn't notice any difference other than "Openclaw is better at finding information in memories". What should reviewers focus on? Look at the interface used between memory-core and MMR. MMR has been moved to a plugin, and the new external reranker uses the same interface.
Summary guidance This PR makes two changes. First, it moves mmr to a bundled plugin called memory-mmr. Second, it adds a memory-external-reranker plugin to memory-core, enabling external reranker support for Cohere-compatible /v1/rerank endpoints. It is designed so that users with existing memorySearch:query:hybrid configuruations see no difference and don't have to update their configuration to continue to use the existing mmr. Only when the user enables and memory-external-reranker plugin and adds the other necessary config entries will the external reranker be used. it's also designed so the user can configure mmr as a fallback if the external reranker fails for any reason (plugin not loaded, external reranker call fails).
## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes #64438 Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
Linked context guidance https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64438
## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: external reranker called during memory recall - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Logs from a running OpenClaw instance showing the external reranker in action: ``` 13:09:30+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] reranking with provider=local-llm-server-02-8082 model=mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 fallbacks=none topN=30 documents=34 13:09:30+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] candidate=local-llm-server-02-8082/mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 model=mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 url=http://local-llm-server-02:8082/v1/rerank topN=30 documents=34 ``` ... ``` 13:09:36+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] candidate=local-llm-server-02-8082/mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 response status=200 ok=true 13:09:36+00:00 debug [memory-external-reranker] candidate=local-llm-server-02-8082/mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0 success: 30 documents reranked ``` The reranker plugin loaded as a gateway plugin, received 34 candidate documents, sent them to the remote endpoint, and returned 30 reranked results. - Observed result after fix: better search results from memory - What was not tested: QMD, LanceDB, other memory systems - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
Real behavior proof guidance config json fragments agents:defaults: ``` "memorySearch": { "enabled": true, "provider": "openai-compatible", "model": "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "query": { "hybrid": { "enabled": true, "vectorWeight": 0.7, "textWeight": 0.3, "mmr": { "enabled": true, "lambda": 0.7, "provider": "memory-external-reranker", "fallback": "memory-mmr" } } }, "remote": { "baseUrl": "http://local-llm-server-02:8081/v1", "apiKey": { "source": "env", "provider": "default", "id": "LOCAL_LLM_02_KEY" }, "batch": { "enabled": false }, "nonBatchConcurrency": 3 }, } ``` models:providers: ``` "local-llm-server-02-8082": { "baseUrl": "http://local-llm-server-02:8082", "models": [ { "id": "mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "name": "mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "input": [ "text" ] } ] } ``` plugins:entries: ``` "memory-external-reranker": { "enabled": true, "config": { "provider": "local-llm-server-02-8082", "model": "mradermacher/Qwen3-Reranker-4B-GGUF:Q8_0", "topN": 30 } }, ``` - Real environment tested: multiple test VMs. I've been running it on my production Openclaw instance for over a week. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enable and configure the memory-external-reranker plugin, index memory, perform a memory search like "What do you remember about the Lucid Air?" See attached screenshot.
## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test pnpm test:extension memory-external-reranker pnpm test:extension memory-mmr What regression coverage was added or updated? Full tests added for both MMR plugin and new external reranker plugin. What failed before this fix, if known? If no test was added, why not?
Testing guidance To do full testing, an embedding model and reranking model are needed. I use llamacpp on localhost and update openclaw.json to point to the local models. ``` ./llama-server -hf Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-GGUF --port 8081 --embedding ./llama-server -hf ggml-org/Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B-Q8_0-GGUF --port 8082 --embedding --reranking --pooling rank ```
## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? No Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes. New config entries added Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No. Uses existing patterns and code to call external reranker. What is the highest-risk area? Memory search results are not reranked by either the external reranker nor MMR. How is that risk mitigated? MMR is a bundled plugin. As long as mmr is a buldled plugin and loaded by default, the risk is minimal.
Risk guidance Minimal risk once MMR is a bundled plugin.
## Current review state What is the next action? What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
Review state guidance Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
## Summary

Isolates QMD mcporter configuration per agent while preserving user/workspace-owned mcporter definitions that must stay external. For agents without an explicit user-owned mcporter server definition, OpenClaw generates a per-agent stdio config under `$STATE/agents/<agentId>/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json` with manager-scoped QMD env vars.

Supersedes #79745. This replacement PR keeps the QMD/mcporter fix and drops the unrelated Skill Workshop i18n commit that accidentally widened the superseding branch.

## Linked context

Related #79745.

## Behavior addressed

- Remote QMD servers with auth material (`headers`, env, URL credentials, refresh metadata, token-like query params) stay external and use the original mcporter config.
- Servers with `lifecycle`, daemon logging, relative `cwd`, or relative `path` are treated as user-owned and kept external.
- Bare stdio QMD servers are rewritten into a generated per-agent config under OpenClaw state.
- `startDaemon=false` omits the keep-alive lifecycle in the generated config so mcporter does not auto-launch the daemon.
- Snake-case remote endpoint keys (`base_url`, `server_url`) are recognized alongside camelCase.
- Project-scoped mcporter configs are probed before legacy home fallback.
- Discovery mode uses a clean `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` so OpenClaw state dirs do not shadow user/project configs.
- External-mode calls set `MCPORTER_CONFIG` to the discovered source path so the original config is reused.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving configured user/workspace mcporter servers that contain auth, lifecycle, logging, cwd/path, or other mcporter-owned behavior.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node v24.15.0, mcporter 0.10.2, qmd 2.1.0 (bab86d5), OpenClaw built from `fix/qmd-mcporter-agent-isolation`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
pnpm build
export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/config"
export XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/xdg-config"
export XDG_CACHE_HOME="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/xdg-cache"
export QMD_CONFIG_DIR="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/qmd-config"
unset MCPORTER_CONFIG

./openclaw.mjs agents add proof-a --non-interactive --workspace "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/workspace-a"
./openclaw.mjs agents add proof-b --non-interactive --workspace "$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/workspace-b"
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.backend qmd
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.command qmd
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.mcporter.enabled true
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.mcporter.serverName qmd
./openclaw.mjs config set memory.qmd.mcporter.startDaemon false

./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "test" --agent proof-a
./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "test" --agent proof-b
```

- Evidence after fix:

Per-agent generated configs are isolated:

```text
$STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json
  command: qmd
  args: ["mcp"]
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/xdg-config
  QMD_CONFIG_DIR: $STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/xdg-config/qmd
  XDG_CACHE_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-a/qmd/xdg-cache
  lifecycle: absent when startDaemon=false

$STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json
  command: qmd
  args: ["mcp"]
  XDG_CONFIG_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/xdg-config
  QMD_CONFIG_DIR: $STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/xdg-config/qmd
  XDG_CACHE_HOME: $STATE/agents/proof-b/qmd/xdg-cache
  lifecycle: absent when startDaemon=false
```

`startDaemon=true` adds the keep-alive lifecycle:

```text
$STATE/agents/proof-daemon/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json
  lifecycle: { mode: "keep-alive", idleTimeoutMs: 300000 }
```

Remote auth config stays external:

```text
User ~/.mcporter/mcporter.json contains:
  base_url: https://qmd.example.com/mcp
  headers.Authorization: Bearer redacted-token

./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "external" --agent proof-external
  mcporter call qmd.query ... failed with ENOTFOUND qmd.example.com

No $STATE/agents/proof-external/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json was generated; auth material was not copied into OpenClaw state.
```

Project config wins over home config:

```text
$WORKSPACE/config/mcporter.json contains qmd cwd ./project-cwd and lifecycle keep-alive.
./openclaw.mjs memory search --query "project" --agent proof-project
  mcporter retries the configured qmd server and preserves the relative cwd from project config.
```

- Observed result after fix: bare stdio QMD servers are isolated per agent, daemon startup follows `startDaemon`, and user-owned remote/project/lifecycle/logging/cwd/path mcporter definitions remain external instead of being copied into generated OpenClaw-managed state.
- What was not tested: Windows paths. Broken explicit `MCPORTER_CONFIG` still silently falls back to generated mode for the default `qmd` server; this is pre-existing behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the remote-auth proof used a redacted/unresolvable test endpoint to prove OpenClaw reused the external mcporter config without copying auth-bearing state.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): previous reviews on #79745 confirmed current main passed QMD-scoped XDG env into mcporter, which could redirect mcporter discovery/state into per-agent QMD paths.

## Tests and validation

Fresh validation after cleaning this superseding PR branch on head `e1ec585278`:

```sh
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts
```

Observed local result:

```text

agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts: 33 tests passed

```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`, in the safer direction: auth-bearing mcporter definitions stay external and are not copied into OpenClaw-managed generated state.

Highest-risk area: mcporter config discovery and upgrade compatibility for existing QMD memory users.

Mitigation: preserve explicit/project/home mcporter config sources when the server definition contains user-owned behavior, generate per-agent configs only for bare stdio QMD servers, and cover the classifier/generation matrix in `qmd-manager.test.ts`.

target.title: fix(agents): drop body/method on cross-origin MCP HTTP redirects
target.body:
## Summary

The MCP HTTP transport passed `allowCrossOriginUnsafeRedirectReplay: true`, so a cross-origin 30x redirect replayed the original POST body/method — an SSRF / credential-exfil vector. Set it to `false` to match the secure default already used by `provider-transport-fetch.ts`, letting the fetch-guard strip body+method on cross-origin redirects.

- Files: src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (release-only per `AGENTS.md`).

## Why core (not ClawHub)
Core security hardening touching `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts` — per `AGENTS.md` these stay in core (security/core hardening, bundled regressions, missing core APIs), not optional skill bundles routed to ClawHub.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts` => **8 passed**
- `pnpm tsgo:core` (tsconfig.core.json) => clean
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on touched files => clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** MCP HTTP transport no longer replays the request body/method across a cross-origin redirect.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 25.3.0); OpenClaw at base 2accfeedc7; Node 22; Vitest via scripts/run-vitest.mjs.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** 8 tests pass, incl. new cases: a cross-origin 307 POST redirect drops body + content-type, and a 302 POST redirect switches to GET and drops the body.
- **Observed result after fix:** Cross-origin redirect on a POST is stripped to a bodyless GET by the SSRF guard; same-origin behavior unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** Full Crabbox/live E2E against a real MCP server over a redirecting proxy; Linux CI; cross-OS.. Independently reviewed by a separate agent before commit; not yet run through Crabbox/$autoreview.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): surface accountId on agent hook context
target.body:
## TL;DR

Adds an optional `accountId` to the plugin **agent hook context** so account-aware plugins can read which channel account a turn belongs to, instead of reverse-engineering it from `sessionKey` (which doesn't carry it for group or cron runs). Fully additive.

**Maintainer decision requested:** `accountId` becomes part of the public plugin SDK hook context. The name mirrors the `accountId` already on the hook *event* types (e.g. `before_agent_run`) — this just brings the *context* in line. OK to lock in `accountId` as the canonical field?

## Why

Multi-account deployments (several Feishu apps / Slack workspaces on one instance) already track an `accountId` internally for routing and auth, but plugins receiving agent hooks have no reliable way to recover it:

- `sessionKey` only encodes the account on some surfaces — **absent** in group conversations (`agent:<id>:<provider>:group:<chatId>`) and cron runs (`agent:main:cron:<job>:run:<id>`).
- Indexing a store by `sessionKey` races when multiple bots are @-ed in one group at the same time.

Surfacing the turn-bound `accountId` at the runtime seam lets a plugin stay **stateless** across DM / group-multi-bot / cron / subagent with a single hook — no sessionKey parsing, no shared-store workaround.

## What changed

- **`PluginHookAgentContext`** — new optional `accountId?: string` (public SDK surface).
- **`buildAgentHookContextChannelFields`** — surfaces `accountId`, following the exact pattern the shared builder already uses for `channel` / `chatId` / `senderId`. Accepts the existing internal `agentAccountId` as an alias so all current spread call sites forward it for free (no rename churn); `accountId` wins. Alias is commented with a follow-up to unify the internal name.
- **Harness path** (`buildAgentHookContext`) — threads `accountId` through, so `agent_end` / compaction / `before_agent_run` hooks get it instead of having it dropped by the context rebuild.
- **Dispatch sites** — `resolve_exec_env` (`bash-tools.exec.ts`) and `before_agent_reply` (`get-reply.ts`) source `accountId` from real adjacent fields, mirroring the sibling `sessionKey` / `channelId` reads.
- **`docs/plugins/hooks.md`** — documents `ctx.accountId`.

10 files, +61/-3.

## Compatibility

Fully additive. Plugins that don't read `accountId` are unaffected; every internal caller keeps working unchanged via the `agentAccountId` alias. `tsgo` core/extensions/test-src all green — no caller type-breaks.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `accountId` was unreachable by plugin agent hooks for group/cron turns (their `sessionKey` carries no account segment); this surfaces it on the hook context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (`openclaw gateway`) on macOS with an isolated state dir, plus the compiled `dist` plugin SDK barrel driven directly via `node`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured --auth none` with a local probe plugin registered.
  - `openclaw cron add --session isolated --account <acct> ...` then `openclaw cron run <jobId>` — a real agent turn whose delivery names a channel account.
  - `node` against the compiled SDK with the cron param shape (imports `buildAgentHookContextChannelFields` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`).
- **Evidence after fix:** copied console output.

  ```
  $ node ./runtime-proof.mjs   # compiled dist SDK, cron isolated-runner param shape
  cron fields: {"channelId":"telegram","accountId":"feishu-proof-account-a"}   # cron sessionKey carries no account, accountId still resolved
  precedence : {"accountId":"acct-canonical"}                                   # canonical accountId wins over legacy agentAccountId alias
  absent     : {"channelId":"discord"}                                          # omitted when the run carries no account
  PROOF OK
  ```

  ```
  $ openclaw cron runs --id <jobId>   # real gateway cron agent turn, persisted record
  "status": "ok", "provider": "...", "sessionKey": "agent:main:cron:<job>:run:<id>",
  "delivery": { "resolved": { "accountId": "feishu-proof-account-a" } }   # account resolved end-to-end through the run
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** the compiled hook-context builder returns the channel account for the cron shape, prefers the canonical `accountId` over the legacy alias, and omits it when no account is present; the real cron turn resolves the same account end-to-end.
- **What was not tested:** a live print of `ctx.accountId` from inside a third-party plugin hook during the turn — that's chicken-and-egg pre-merge (needs this change shipped in a gateway with a consuming plugin). It exercises the same builder the live dispatch uses; happy to add that capture as a follow-up once it lands.

## Verification

- `tsgo` core / extensions / test-src lanes: green.
- New unit coverage: `hook-agent-context.test.ts` (accountId surfacing / `agentAccountId` alias / canonical precedence) and `hook-context.test.ts` (harness ctx carries vs. omits accountId — guards the `agent_end` rebuild path).
- Adjacent hook callsite suites green: `get-reply.before-agent-reply`, `bash-tools.exec.resolve-env-hook`, `cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron`, `harness/agent-end-side-effects`, `harness/lifecycle-hook-helpers`.
- `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, docs format check clean. Rebased on latest `main`; CI green.

Refs #87668

target.title: fix(config): accept transport:stdio in MCP server schema

Add "stdio" to the MCP server transport enum in the config schema so

servers. The Zod schema only allows `sse | streamable-http`, but the

`src/config/zod-schema.ts` transport union missing `"stdio"` literal.

- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — one line: add `z.literal("stdio")` to
  the transport union

## Related

Closes #95082
