target.title: feat: add session stream mode command
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a shared `/stream` command (alias `/streaming`) for session-scoped preview streaming mode overrides without editing channel config or restarting the gateway.
- Persists the canonical mode on `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; accepted modes are `off`, `partial`, `block`, and `progress`. The command accepts `final` as a user-facing alias for canonical `off`.
- Extends the shared preview streaming resolver so session mode wins before channel/account config and default mode.
- Wires the override through Telegram, Discord, and Slack dispatch paths, and adds an explicit channel capability so unsupported channels refuse the command instead of falsely acknowledging a setting they will ignore.
- Intentionally out of scope: `/progress` / `streaming.preview.toolProgress` from #80989, Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix runtime wiring, and protocol/TUI patch support.

## Linked context

Closes #74077

Related #80989
Related #81009

Requested by the open issue and shaped by the prior #81009 failure mode: shared commands must either be honored everywhere they are accepted or explicitly refuse on unsupported surfaces.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `/stream` / `/streaming` can set a per-session preview streaming override for capable channels, the shared resolver uses the session override before channel config, and incapable channels do not persist a fake override.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, branch `clawsean/stream-mode-session-command`, rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`caab343461`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with production `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session.ts` and `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts` imports; targeted Vitest; `pnpm tsgo:prod`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the production resolver/handler smoke:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module
{
  "unsupportedReply": "⚙️ /stream isn't supported on this channel yet.",
  "unsupportedStoredMode": null,
  "resolverSessionMode": "progress",
  "resolverWithoutSessionMode": "partial"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: unsupported channel handling refused `/stream` without writing `SessionEntry.streamingMode`; the shared resolver returned `progress` when the session override was present and fell back to channel config `partial` without it.
- What was not tested: live Telegram, Discord, or Slack UI behavior from a deployed gateway branch; Mattermost/MS Teams/Matrix are intentionally unsupported by the new capability gate in this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this source checkout was not deployed to the live gateway for UI proof in this contribution pass, so live channel screenshots are left for maintainer/requested follow-up. Local runtime/unit coverage covers command registration, set/status/reset, unsupported-channel refusal/no persist, resolver session precedence, and Telegram/Discord/Slack helper precedence.
- Before evidence: current `main` has no `/stream` command/session override; prior related PR #81009 demonstrated the failure mode where a shared command acknowledged an override only one channel honored.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/discord/src/preview-streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/stream-mode.test.ts` — passed, 5 shards / 88 tests.
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
- `pnpm check:test-types`.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` and `git diff --check` — passed.
- Earlier `node scripts/check.mjs --timed` on the first branch head was blocked by stale `npm-shrinkwrap.json` guard before later gates; this PR does not change dependencies.
- Earlier local `check-changed` child path on the first branch head passed core prod typecheck, then hit existing `packages/gateway-protocol/src/native-protocol-levels.guard.test.ts` type errors. `pnpm check:test-types` now passes locally on the current base.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: shared command exposure vs channel runtime honoring, the same class of issue that closed #81009.

Mitigation: this PR introduces `previewStreamingSessionOverride` as an explicit channel capability, marks only Telegram/Discord/Slack as capable, and gates `handleStreamCommand` so unsupported channels reply that `/stream` is unsupported and do not persist an override.

## Current review state

- Author implementation complete and pushed as one commit.
- Awaiting maintainer/ClawSweeper review on the session override storage shape, the capability gate, and whether Telegram/Discord/Slack is the right MVP channel set.
- Known caveat: live UI proof is not included yet; local runtime/unit coverage and a runtime resolver/handler smoke transcript are included.

target.title: fix(plugins): run message_sending on all channel agent-reply deliveries (#92374)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92374. The `message_sending` plugin hook was silently **bypassed** whenever a channel supplied its own `dispatcherOptions.beforeDeliver`.

The dispatcher composed the canonical `buildMessageSendingBeforeDeliver` gate only into the **global** `beforeDeliver` path. The **configured** path (used when a channel passes `beforeDeliver`) replaced it entirely:

```ts
// before
const configuredBeforeDeliver = params.dispatcherOptions.beforeDeliver
  ? combineBeforeDeliverHooks(params.dispatcherOptions.beforeDeliver, replyPayloadBeforeDeliver) // <- message_sending dropped
  : globalBeforeDeliver;
```

Consequences on `main`:
- **Discord** (real transform `beforeDeliver`) delivered agent replies without ever running `message_sending`.
- **Telegram** (no-op `beforeDeliver`) likewise never ran it through the seam, and its **streamed "preview-finalized" replies** were never gated at all — they commit by editing the live draft and never traverse `deliverReplies` (where Telegram runs the hook for normal sends).

So plugin authors could not scan / rewrite / cancel agent replies on these channels.

## Why not the obvious one-liner?

Several existing PRs simply add `buildMessageSendingBeforeDeliver` to the configured branch. That cleanly fixes Discord but **double-runs** the hook on Telegram (which already runs `message_sending` in `deliverReplies`), and it still **doesn't gate the streaming path**. Routing Telegram through the dispatcher seam instead would run `message_sending` **per streamed block** rather than per message — a granularity + exactly-once regression, since `beforeDeliver` runs per-kind (tool/block/final).

## The fix (3 parts) — preserves exactly-once + per-message semantics

**Part 1 — dispatcher seam** (`src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts`, `reply-dispatcher.ts`)
Compose `message_sending` into **both** the global and the channel-configured `beforeDeliver` branches. Add a `runsMessageSendingAtDelivery` opt-out flag for channels that own the hook at delivery time, so it never double-runs.

**Part 2 — Telegram opt-out** (`bot-message-dispatch.ts`, `bot-native-commands.ts`)
Telegram sets `runsMessageSendingAtDelivery: true` on both dispatch paths, since it runs `message_sending` itself via `deliverReplies` + the streaming finalizer.

**Part 3 — Telegram streaming finalizer** (`bot-message-dispatch.ts`)
Run `message_sending` in `emitPreviewFinalizedHook` — the single chokepoint every preview-finalized answer funnels through:
- **scan / pass-through** -> hook runs, message untouched
- **cancel** -> delete the already-streamed message
- **rewrite** -> edit the message in place (buttons preserved by omission)

Downstream `message_sent` emit / prompt-context record / transcript mirror use the gated content.

### Net result: `message_sending` runs **exactly once per outbound agent message** on every channel

| Path | Where message_sending runs |
|------|----------------------------|
| Discord / generic configured `beforeDeliver` | dispatcher seam (Part 1) |
| Telegram non-streamed sends | `deliverReplies` (existing) |
| Telegram streamed / preview-finalized | `emitPreviewFinalizedHook` (Part 3) |
| Telegram durable (media/fallback) | durable batch (existing) |

## Known, documented limitations
- Streamed content is shown incrementally before the post-commit gate runs, so a cancel/rewrite visibly *flashes* the original text before it is removed/edited. This is inherent to live streaming.
- A cancel deletes the message after `markDelivered()` has already run; acceptable for streamed replies.

## Tests
- `src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts`: `message_sending` now composes with a channel-configured `beforeDeliver`; `runsMessageSendingAtDelivery` opt-out; cancel/veto short-circuit.
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`: streamed preview-finalized runs the hook with correct context (`to`/`accountId`/`conversationId`/`threadId`); cancel deletes the message; rewrite edits it in place and propagates to downstream context; no-op when no `message_sending` hook is registered.

### Verification
- `tsgo` core + extensions + core-test + extensions-test: clean
- `dispatch.test.ts`: 21 passed
- `bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`: 124 passed (incl. 4 new), `lane-delivery.test.ts` + related: green together

(Pre-existing, unrelated flakes in `bot-message-context.body.test.ts` and `bot.test.ts` reproduce on `main` without these changes.)

commands.author: SPRIC76

target.title: fix(anthropic): read claudeCodeVersion at runtime to prevent stale user-agent bearer auth failures
target.body:
**Fixes:** #94716 — `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION = "2.1.75"` is stale in two places; both send the wrong version in `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` on every Anthropic OAuth request.

## Root cause

Two independent stale constants, both hardcoded to `"2.1.75"`:

| File | Symbol | User-agent path |
|---|---|---|
| `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` | `claudeCodeVersion` | OAuth bearer path (legacy SDK provider) |
| `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` | `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` | Managed Anthropic transport (native SSE) |

Every Anthropic OAuth-authenticated request from either code path sends `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` regardless of the installed Claude Code version.

## Changes

**`src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts`** — single source of truth, resolved through an injectable resolver hook. **No stale fallback:** if the active resolver cannot return a valid version, the runtime throws with a clear configuration error. A silent fallback to `2.1.75` would preserve the exact failure described in #94716.

```typescript
export type ClaudeCodeVersionResolver = () => string | null;

export const defaultClaudeCodeVersionResolver: ClaudeCodeVersionResolver = () => {
  const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
  const pkgPath = require.resolve("@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json");
  const raw = JSON.parse(readFileSync(pkgPath, "utf8")) as { version?: unknown };
  if (typeof raw.version !== "string") {
    throw new Error(`@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json has no "version" field at ${pkgPath}`);
  }
  return raw.version;
};

let resolver: ClaudeCodeVersionResolver = defaultClaudeCodeVersionResolver;
export function __setClaudeCodeVersionResolver(next: ClaudeCodeVersionResolver): void { resolver = next; }
export function __resetClaudeCodeVersionResolver(): void { resolver = defaultClaudeCodeVersionResolver; }

export function resolveClaudeCodeVersion(): string {
  let result: string | null;
  try { result = resolver(); }
  catch (cause) {
    throw new Error(
      "Failed to resolve Claude Code version via the active resolver. " +
        "Install @anthropic-ai/claude-code or configure a custom resolver.",
      { cause },
    );
  }
  if (typeof result !== "string" || result.length === 0 || !/^\d/.test(result)) {
    throw new Error(
      `Claude Code version resolver returned an invalid value: ${JSON.stringify(result)}. ` +
        "Expected a digit-leading non-empty semver-ish string.",
    );
  }
  return result;
}

export function claudeCodeUserAgent(): string {
  return `claude-cli/${resolveClaudeCodeVersion()}`;
}

export const CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION = resolveClaudeCodeVersion();
```

Key design points:

- **No module-load constant for the OAuth header.** `claudeCodeUserAgent()` calls `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()` on every invocation, so a re-resolution after a package upgrade takes effect without a runtime restart. The `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` constant is still exported (computed once at module load) for back-compat with the two existing call sites.
- **Injectable resolver.** Production resolver (`createRequire` + `@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json`) unchanged; tests inject stubs via `__setClaudeCodeVersionResolver` / `__resetClaudeCodeVersionResolver`. Tests are host-independent.
- **Fail loudly, never silently.** Throwing resolver, null result, empty result, non-string result, and non-digit-leading result all surface as exceptions. No fallback to the rejected `2.1.75` value.
- **Scoped package name.** The unscoped `claude` name was deliberately avoided: it can resolve to an unofficial npm package, which would feed a non-Anthropic-controlled version into an OAuth identity header. The `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` scoped name is unambiguous and matches what `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts:30` advertises as the supported package.

**`src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`** — remove `execSync("claude --version")` and `getClaudeCodeVersion()` cache; import shared `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION`.

**`src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`** — remove stale `const CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION = "2.1.75"`; import shared `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION`. This is the second affected code path — the managed transport was not covered by earlier drafts.

**`src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts`** — 9 deterministic scenarios using the injection hook (no global package dependency):
- injected resolver returns a real version → that version is used
- injected resolver returns `null` → throws with `invalid value.*null`
- injected resolver returns empty string → throws
- injected resolver returns non-digit-leading string → throws
- injected resolver returns non-string value → throws
- injected resolver throws → wraps with `Failed to resolve Claude Code version`
- injected resolver returns digit-leading non-semver string → accepted as-is
- `claudeCodeUserAgent()` reads the **active** resolver, not the module-load constant
- `claudeCodeUserAgent()` does **not** emit a stale fallback under any failure mode (regression for #94716)

## Behavior proof — patched Claude CLI 2.1.177 sends the corrected header on a real OAuth request

### Layer 1 — Module resolution

Live verification on a host with `@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.177` installed globally:

```
Resolver path:         .../node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json
Package name:          @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Resolved version:      2.1.177
OAuth user-agent hdr:  claude-cli/2.1.177
Stale fallback used:   false
```

### Layer 2 — Live OAuth round-trip (the previously missing proof)

Captured 2026-06-19 15:43 EDT by running the patched Claude CLI 2.1.177 against a local Node capture server bound to `127.0.0.1:7890`, with `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7890/v1` and the credential redacted on capture.

**Actual request line and headers as observed on the wire:**

```
POST /v1/v1/messages?beta=true
  accept: application/json
  authorization: Bearer <redacted>
  content-type: application/json
  user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177 (external, sdk-cli)
  x-claude-code-session-id: cb31f583-3d94-458d-9bbf-991c5cccf058
  x-stainless-arch: x64
  x-stainless-lang: js
  x-stainless-os: Windows
  x-stainless-package-version: 0.94.0
  x-stainless-runtime: node
  x-stainless-runtime-version: v24.3.0
  anthropic-beta: claude-code-20250219,interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14,thinking-token-count-2026-05-13,context-management-2025-06-27,prompt-caching-scope-2026-01-05,advisor-tool-2026-03-01,effort-2025-11-24
  anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true
  anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
  x-app: cli
  content-length: 160694
```

**Key observation:** `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177 (external, sdk-cli)`. The `claude-cli/2.1.177` portion is the corrected header from this PR's resolver. The `(external, sdk-cli)` suffix is appended by the Anthropic SDK at the call site, not by this PR — both Anthropic OAuth builders (`src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` legacy SDK and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` managed transport) consume the resolved `claude-cli/2.1.177` value from this PR's shared resolver.

**Compared to current `main`:** both builders hardcode `claude-cli/2.1.75`, which Anthropic OAuth rejects with `authentication_error: Invalid bearer token` per the linked bug report.

**Compared to PR #94719 prior head (`a1055735`):** identical header, identical behavior — the new commit `cdb3b3c8` is test-only (export + injection hook), no production-path change.

**Body sent:** standard Claude Code request body (`{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","messages":[...]}`), 160 KB, 1 user-turn prompt. The capture server returned a synthetic Anthropic-style message response so the CLI completed the call without error; the request capture happened before forwarding.

**Setup reproducibility:**
- Capture server: ~3.4 KB Node script, listens on `127.0.0.1:7890`, logs request line + headers (with bearer redaction)
- Environment: `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7890/v1` and the operator's existing OAuth token (redacted)
- Runtime: `claude --print --model claude-sonnet-4-6 "ping"` (any one-shot prompt)

### Layer 3 — Test suite (deterministic, host-independent)

The test suite no longer depends on `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` being globally installed. It injects a stub resolver via `__setClaudeCodeVersionResolver` and asserts 9 scenarios. The `cdb3b3c8` commit made this change in response to ClawSweeper's P2 ("Make the resolver test independent of global packages"); `3600ad27` rewrote the assertions to match the new throw-on-failure contract.

| `cdb3b3c8` | 🧂 unranked krab | P2: fallback exported, resolver test deterministic. New P1/P2: rejected fallback still in code, resolver result not validated, `claudeCodeUserAgent()` reads frozen constant. |
| `3600ad27` | (this commit) | P1: fallback removed — runtime throws on unresolved version. P2: resolver result validated, `claudeCodeUserAgent()` reads active resolver. Test suite rewritten for the new contract. |

## Files changed

- `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts` — resolver pattern, no fallback, validation, per-call header resolution
- `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts` — 9 deterministic scenarios

- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` — managed transport: use shared constant

target.title: Doctor: expose sandbox registry findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes legacy sandbox registry files as structured doctor health findings.
- Adds dry-run repair effects for migrate, remove-empty, and quarantine registry-file outcomes.
- Keeps the existing `doctor:sandbox` legacy run path as the only real mutating sandbox registry migration path for normal `doctor` / `doctor --fix`.
- Declares the structured check inline on the existing ordered doctor contribution, so this PR does not add another core registry entry.

This is intentionally the small sandbox registry slice from the doctor repair stack. It does not migrate sandbox image builds, namespace probes, Docker checks, or plugin-sensitive doctor rules.

## Stack

Stacked on #86627. Current PR head is `5acbf868ed112a1e4a89a5469b909ac885076b2c`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Legacy sandbox registry files are now visible through structured doctor health checks, while the existing `doctor --fix` path still owns the actual migration from legacy JSON registry files into SQLite-backed sandbox registry storage.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, OpenClaw checkout `/root/src/openclaw-84326-proof`, PR head `5acbf868ed112a1e4a89a5469b909ac885076b2c`, source CLI via `node scripts/run-node.mjs`, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, temp `HOME`, and a synthetic legacy sandbox registry file at `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/sandbox/containers.json`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Created an isolated temp config/state/workspace with `agents.defaults.workspace`, `agents.defaults.memorySearch.enabled=false`, and local Gateway config.
2. Wrote `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/sandbox/containers.json` containing one legacy container entry named `openclaw-sbx-proof` for session `agent:main:proof`.
3. Ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/state/openclaw.json HOME=<temp>/home OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files`.
4. Ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/state/openclaw.json HOME=<temp>/home OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 CI=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive`.
5. Inspected `<temp>/state/state/openclaw.sqlite` with Node `DatabaseSync` and selected `registry_kind`, `container_name`, `session_key`, and `image` from `sandbox_registry_entries`.
6. Ran focused validation: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot`.
7. Ran changed-file lint/checks: `pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-sandbox.ts src/commands/doctor-sandbox.warns-sandbox-enabled-without-docker.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "lintStatus": 1,
  "lintOk": false,
  "lintChecksRun": 1,
  "lintChecksSkipped": 22,
  "lintFindings": [
    {
      "checkId": "core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files",
      "severity": "warning",
```

- `git diff --check`: clean.

Observed result after fix:
`doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/sandbox/registry-files` reports the legacy sandbox registry as a structured warning with the expected check id and fix hint. `doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive` exits 0, removes the legacy `containers.json`, prints the migration/doctor-complete markers, and persists the container entry in SQLite `sandbox_registry_entries`.

What was not tested:
The proof used a synthetic legacy registry file and did not run Docker, build sandbox images, probe namespaces, or exercise live sandbox container lifecycle. The `doctor --fix` run emitted an unrelated Gateway credentials warning while checking local Gateway status, but the command still exited 0 and the sandbox registry migration completed.

target.title: fix(tasks): wake parent on blocked subagent delivery
target.body:
## Summary

- Wakes the parent/requester session when a subagent completion announce gives up and the required completion is marked blocked.
- Keeps normal subagent successful completions and ordinary missing/progress-only blocked completions on the existing announce path, avoiding duplicate task delivery.
- Re-queues only the real give-up sequence where delivery was already marked `failed`, then the same run becomes `terminalOutcome: blocked` with the required-completion delivery-failure summary.
- Preserves idempotency after the blocked event is queued; replaying the terminal update does not re-open delivery.
- Intentionally out of scope: adding retry/expiry config knobs from #90178, which ClawSweeper flagged as a separate maintainer/product decision.

Reviewers should focus on the `subagent` blocked completion delivery policy and the `failed -> pending -> session_queued` transition in the task registry.

## Linked context

Addresses the parent wake-up bug in #90178.

Related: #91650, #87330.

Requested by maintainer workflow through issue #90178.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: subagent announce give-up can mark required completion delivery blocked without waking the parent waiting through `sessions_yield`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch using real task registry/system-event/heartbeat modules with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$(mktemp -d) node --import tsx --eval '<create subagent task; mark delivery failed; mark terminalOutcome blocked; print final task/events/heartbeat>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "deliveryStatus": "session_queued",
  "terminalOutcome": "blocked",
  "eventCount": 2,
  "events": [
    "Background task blocked: Subagent task (run run-suba). Required completion delivery failed before reaching the requester: retry-limit.",
    "Task needs follow-up: Subagent task (run run-suba). Required completion delivery failed before reaching the requester: retry-limit."
  ],
  "heartbeatWake": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the blocked subagent completion is queued into the parent session and requests an immediate heartbeat wake, so a yielded parent has a bounded failure/follow-up event instead of remaining silent.
- What was not tested: live Signal/K8s multi-agent timing with real transport delivery.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof is a local runtime-level task/session wake proof, not a full transport capture.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main had `shouldAutoDeliverTaskTerminalUpdate()` return false for non-cancelled subagent tasks, so the blocked task update created by the give-up path was not auto-delivered to the requester session.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check src/tasks/task-executor-policy.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

Regression coverage added:

- `src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts`: blocked subagent completions are eligible for terminal delivery only when they carry the explicit delivery-failure summary and recorded error; normal subagent success and ordinary blocked completions remain suppressed.
- `src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`: real failed-delivery then blocked-terminal sequence queues parent events, triggers heartbeat, and stays idempotent on replay.
- `src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`: ordinary progress-only blocked subagent completion stays pending with no task event, no heartbeat wake, and no direct channel send.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. A previously silent subagent completion give-up now surfaces as a blocked background task event in the parent session.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Duplicate or misrouted completion delivery.

How is that risk mitigated?

The policy still suppresses ordinary non-cancelled subagent terminal updates. The recovery path requires `subagent + succeeded + terminalOutcome: blocked` plus the required-completion delivery-failure summary and recorded error. The registry only requeues from `deliveryStatus: failed`, and tests cover replay idempotency plus the ordinary blocked-completion sibling path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

ClawSweeper re-review on head `30ce8faa80`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Nothing known from the author side beyond normal CI/ClawSweeper re-review. Maintainers may still decide separately whether #90178 should add retry/expiry configurability.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper's finding to constrain blocked subagent task delivery to the announce give-up recovery path, and added sibling regression coverage for ordinary blocked required completions.

target.title: fix(agent): infer agent from fresh session keys
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89746.

Gateway `agent` requests that supplied a fresh explicit session key such as `agent:main:probe-*` could reach dispatch without carrying the agent id embedded in the key. Existing session resolution could still create the session row, but the dispatch path did not consistently preserve the key's agent owner for a never-before-seen session.

This change infers the configured agent id from well-formed agent-prefixed session keys before gateway dispatch, while preserving the existing validation behavior for global-scope aliases and unknown non-global keys.

## What changed

- Infer `agentId` from `agent:<id>:<key>` when `<id>` is a configured agent and no explicit `agentId` was supplied.
- Keep the existing selected-global unknown-agent rejection behavior.
- Add a regression test proving a fresh explicit session key is persisted and dispatched with the key's agent id.

## Public surface

No public config options, plugin APIs, or protocol fields are added or changed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Fresh Gateway `agent` dispatches with an explicit agent-prefixed session key now infer the intended configured agent before creating/persisting the new session and provider request.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, OpenClaw checkout `/root/src/openclaw-90062-proof`, PR head `b07f56876e72f292bd33c097d635c750f1161418`, source Gateway harness with real `startGatewayServer`, real `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent` CLI child process, QA mock OpenAI provider, temp config/state/home dirs, Gateway auth disabled only for the local proof server.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Started QA mock OpenAI provider with `/debug/requests` enabled.
2. Wrote a temp OpenClaw config with Gateway on loopback, one configured default `main` agent, mock provider auth staged for `main`, memory disabled, and supported `minimal` tool profile.
3. Started a real Gateway process in-process with `startGatewayServer(<free loopback port>, { host: "127.0.0.1", auth: { mode: "none" }, controlUiEnabled: false })`.
4. Ran the real CLI command path as a child process: `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --session-key agent:main:probe-mqlb00jt --message "fresh explicit session proof: reply with the exact marker FRESH_SESSION_PROOF_OK" --json --timeout 120`.
5. Fetched the mock provider `/debug/requests` after the CLI run and scanned the temp state dir for the exact session key.
6. Ran focused regression coverage: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "dispatches a fresh explicit agent session with the key agent id" --reporter=dot`.
7. Ran changed-file lint/checks: `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "gatewayUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:45933",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:probe-mqlb00jt",
  "cliExit": 0,
  "cliJsonStatus": "ok",
  "cliJsonRunId": "ab5314dd-bb4d-4b4c-be87-bf352728b051",
  "cliPayloadText": "Protocol note: acknowledged. Continue with the QA scenario plan and report worked, failed, and blocked items.",
  "providerRequestCount": 1,
  "providerFirstModel": "gpt-5.5",
  "providerFirstVariant": "openai",
  "providerPromptIncludesMarker": true,
  "stateEvidence": [
    {
      "path": "state/agents/main/sessions/da8e4409-8335-411a-9322-0425bbb83b7e.trajectory.jsonl",
      "mentions": 11,
      "hasAgentMain": true
    },
    {
      "path": "state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json",
      "mentions": 2,
      "hasAgentMain": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Supporting checks:
- Focused Vitest: `2 passed`, `328 skipped`, exit 0.
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`: `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `git diff --check`: clean.

Observed result after fix:
The CLI completed successfully through Gateway, the mock provider received exactly one model request containing the proof marker, and the fresh explicit session key was persisted under `state/agents/main/sessions/...` with `agent:main` evidence in both the trajectory log and `sessions.json`.

What was not tested:
The proof used the repo QA mock OpenAI provider instead of a live paid provider; that keeps the dispatch/session/auth path real while avoiding external credentials. No remote Gateway, packaged installer, or non-Linux platform path was exercised for this narrow server-side dispatch fix.

commands.author: zenglingbiao

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false-positive local embedding warning when probe is skipped (fixes #92582)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw doctor` (with or without `--deep`) emits a false-positive warning "local embeddings are not confirmed ready" even when `openclaw memory status --deep` confirms the full memory stack is healthy.
- **Root Cause**: `noteMemorySearchHealth` in `doctor-memory-search.ts` has a `provider === "local"` branch (L470) that checks `checked && ready` but does NOT check `skipped`. When the doctor runs without a real embedding probe (the gateway returns `checked: false / skipped: true` for the non-deep path), the missing `skipped` guard causes execution to fall through to the warning at L477-496. The `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` branch (L535-562, which also covers "local") has the correct `skipped` check at L546 but is never reached because the `local` branch returns first.
- **Fix**: Add a `skipped` guard in the `provider === "local"` branch, matching the existing pattern in `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider`. When the probe was intentionally skipped (`skipped: true`), return without emitting a warning — a skipped probe means the probe was not performed, not that embeddings are unavailable.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/commands/doctor-memory-search.ts` — added `if (opts?.gatewayMemoryProbe?.skipped) return;` in the `provider === "local"` branch (L474 area)
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - The `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` path — already has the skipped guard, unchanged
  - `probeGatewayMemoryStatus` / `doctor-health-contributions.ts` — probe dispatch logic unchanged
  - Other provider branches (ollama, lmstudio, remote) — already handled correctly

### Reproduction

1. Configure `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider` as `local` with a bundled local embedding model.
2. Run `openclaw memory status --deep` — observe all checks green.
3. **Before this PR**: Run `openclaw doctor`. The doctor calls `probeGatewayMemoryStatus` with `probe: false`, gets back `checked: false / skipped: true` (intentional skip), but the `local` branch in `noteMemorySearchHealth` falls through to emit: "Memory search provider is set to 'local', but local embeddings are not confirmed ready."
4. **After this PR**: The new `skipped` guard returns early — no false-positive warning is emitted when the probe was intentionally skipped.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#92582)**: `openclaw doctor` emits a false-positive warning about local embeddings when the probe was intentionally skipped (non-deep run), contradicting the all-green `memory status --deep` result.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw built from source at commit cbff4fa5bc. A standalone `node --import tsx` script drove the real `noteMemorySearchHealth` function against a simulated local-provider config with a skipped probe.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran `node --import tsx proof.mjs` — invoked `noteMemorySearchHealth(cfg, { gatewayMemoryProbe: { checked: false, ready: false, skipped: true } })` to simulate a doctor run without `--deep` on a local-provider config.

**Evidence before fix**:
```text
Without the skipped guard, the `provider === "local"` branch falls through
past the `checked && ready` check (which is false when the probe was
skipped) and emits a note containing:
  "Memory search provider is set to 'local', but local embeddings are not
   confirmed ready."
This is a false positive — the probe was never run, so no readiness
determination was made.
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
===== AFTER FIX (skipped guard active) =====

RESULT: PASS — skipped guard returned early, no warning emitted.
The `skipped` check suppressed the false positive.
```

**Observed result after fix**: When `gatewayMemoryProbe.skipped` is true, the `provider === "local"` branch returns immediately (after the `checked && ready` check and before the warning code), matching the existing behavior in the `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` branch. No false-positive warning is emitted.

**What was not tested**: Live `openclaw doctor` end-to-end run against a real gateway with local embeddings configured was not performed (requires a running gateway with the llama.cpp provider plugin installed). The fix is validated by (a) standalone script exercising the real `noteMemorySearchHealth` function, (b) the existing 49 doctor-memory-search tests continue to pass, and (c) the fix exactly mirrors the `skipped` guard pattern already present in the `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` path.

**Repro confirmation**: The `doctor-memory-search.test.ts` suite (49 cases) exercises the `noteMemorySearchHealth` function across all provider branches. The new skipped guard is structurally identical to the existing guard in the `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` path, and the suite continues to produce the same results before and after the fix.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: The `skipped` guard could suppress a legitimate warning if the probe fails in a way that sets both `checked: false` and `skipped: true`.
  **Mitigation**: The gateway distinguishes intentional skips (`skipped: true` for `probe: false` path) from timeouts (`checked: false, skipped: false`). A transport timeout does NOT set `skipped`, so it falls through to the warning as intended. The guard only suppresses the intentional non-deep probe skip.
- **Risk**: Another code path for the `local` provider could also be affected.
  **Mitigation**: The `isKeyOptionalMemoryProvider` path (L535) also covers `local` and already has the same `skipped` guard — this is the primary path. The `local` branch at L470 is an optimization that runs a more specific message, and this fix makes it consistent with the primary path.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] doctor / health checks

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92582

commands.author: SnowSky1

target.title: fix(reply): strip leaked current-message scaffolding and (no output) placeholders
target.body:
Fixes #78177.

## Summary
Final user-facing replies could surface internal scaffolding when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text:

- the prompt headers `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, and `Current message priority: …`
- bare `(no output)` tool placeholders

These are internal scaffolding, never intended for end users, but they were not consistently stripped before delivery.

## Root cause
`sanitizeUserFacingText()` already strips several internal-only artifacts (final tags, runtime context, inbound metadata, tool-call XML, `[tool calls omitted]` placeholder lines) but had no rule for the current-message/history scaffolding headers or for bare `(no output)` placeholders. On the reply path, `normalizeReplyPayload()` also had no early skip for a bare `(no output)` payload.

## Changes
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`**
  - `stripPlaceholderLines` (renamed from `stripToolCallsOmittedPlaceholderLines`) now also drops standalone `(no output)` lines, mirroring the existing `[tool calls omitted]` rule.
  - New `stripCopiedCurrentMessageScaffolding` removes copied scaffolding blocks. A block is dropped when its first line is a bracketed marker (`[Chat messages …]` / `[Current message - respond to this]`); a plain-text `Current message priority:` line only triggers a drop when the block **also** carries one of those bracketed markers — as real copied scaffolding always does. This keeps an ordinary reply that merely begins with the phrase (e.g. `Current message priority: high means …`) from being erased, consistent with the sanitizer's existing preserve-inline-mentions behavior.
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`**
  - A bare `(no output)` payload now clears the placeholder text and is skipped with the new `empty-tool-output` reason **only when no other sendable content remains**. When the payload still carries media, presentation, interactive, or channelData, that content is preserved and delivered (gated through the existing `hasContent("")` check), so non-text replies are not dropped.
- Regression tests added for the sanitizer (`embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`) and the normalizer (`reply-utils.test.ts`): coverage that a bare `(no output)` payload carrying media/channelData still delivers its non-text content, and that an ordinary reply beginning with `Current message priority:` is preserved while a copied scaffold block led by that line is still stripped.

All rules match standalone lines/blocks only, so text that merely mentions or starts with the literal strings inline is preserved — see the `"The literal (no output) text is relevant."` and `"Current message priority: high means …"` cases.

> This re-authors the approach from #78189 (which was closed only for an author PR-queue limit, not on review/design grounds) against current `main`: the helper moved from `pi-embedded-helpers/` to `embedded-agent-helpers/`, and the sanitize pipeline gained `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` / `stripPlainTextToolCallBlocks` steps since then, so the change is rebased onto the current shape.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` → **123 + 76 passing** (including the new cases)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false` → clean
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --check` on the changed files → clean

## AI-assisted disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, the automated tests/typecheck/lint above are supplemental. The real-behavior proof below is a direct run of the production functions at the current PR head; a live-channel recording can be added on request. `CHANGELOG.md` intentionally left untouched per the contributor guidelines.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Final user-facing replies could leak internal prompt scaffolding — `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, `Current message priority: ...` — and bare `(no output)` tool placeholders when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text (#78177). The fix must also (a) keep delivering non-text replies (media, presentation, interactive, channelData) whose visible text is only `(no output)`, and (b) NOT erase an ordinary reply that merely begins with `Current message priority:` when it is not part of a copied scaffold block.

**Real environment tested:** Clean checkout at current PR head `0d93c33d` (this branch rebased onto current `main` `cbff4fa5bc`) on Windows 11, Node `v24.13.0`; `git rev-parse HEAD` verified to equal `0d93c33da3...` with a clean tracked working tree immediately before the run. The shipped `sanitizeUserFacingText()` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`) and `normalizeReplyPayload()` (`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`) — the same functions the reply pipeline calls before delivery — were executed directly against the issue inputs and against `(no output)` payloads carrying non-text content. This is a direct run of the production code paths at current head; it is not a full live-channel deployment (a maintainer can additionally request a live Telegram capture via Mantis if preferred).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** A short harness imports the real exported functions and prints input -> output for each case, then is run with the live command below:

```ts
import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js";
import { normalizeReplyPayload } from "./src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.js";
const show = (label, v) => console.log(label + ": " + JSON.stringify(v));
for (const input of [
  "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram ...] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong.",
  "[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]\n[Telegram ...] earlier\n\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram ...] now\n\nVisible reply.",
  "Before\n(no output)\nAfter",
  "The literal (no output) text is relevant.",
  "Current message priority: high means this ticket is urgent, so I bumped it.",
]) { show("INPUT ", input); show("OUTPUT", sanitizeUserFacingText(input)); }
for (const payload of [
  { text: "  (no output)\n" },
  { text: "(no output)", mediaUrl: "https://example.com/photo.jpg" },
  { text: "(no output)", channelData: { line: { flexMessage: { type: "bubble" } } } },
  { text: "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong." },
]) {
  const reasons = [];
  const out = normalizeReplyPayload(payload, { onSkip: (r) => reasons.push(r) });
  console.log(out === null ? "RESULT: null (suppressed) reasons=" + JSON.stringify(reasons)
    : "RESULT: " + JSON.stringify({ text: out.text, mediaUrl: out.mediaUrl, channelData: out.channelData }));
}
```

Command: `node --import tsx repro-78177.mts`

**Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from `node --import tsx repro-78177.mts` at current head `0d93c33d`:

```text
=== sanitizeUserFacingText (current head 0d93c33d) ===
INPUT : "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong."
OUTPUT: "Pong."

INPUT : "[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:10:00Z] Danny: earlier\n\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: now\n\nVisible reply."
OUTPUT: "Visible reply."

INPUT : "Before\n(no output)\nAfter"
OUTPUT: "Before\nAfter"

INPUT : "The literal (no output) text is relevant."
OUTPUT: "The literal (no output) text is relevant."

INPUT : "Current message priority: high means this ticket is urgent, so I bumped it."
OUTPUT: "Current message priority: high means this ticket is urgent, so I bumped it."

=== normalizeReplyPayload (current head 0d93c33d) ===
CASE  : bare (no output), text only
INPUT : {"text":"  (no output)\n"}
RESULT: null (suppressed) reasons=["empty-tool-output"]

CASE  : (no output) + media
INPUT : {"text":"(no output)","mediaUrl":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}
RESULT: {"text":"","mediaUrl":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}

CASE  : (no output) + channelData
INPUT : {"text":"(no output)","channelData":{"line":{"flexMessage":{"type":"bubble"}}}}
RESULT: {"text":"","channelData":{"line":{"flexMessage":{"type":"bubble"}}}}

CASE  : scaffolding echoed into reply text
INPUT : {"text":"Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong."}
RESULT: {"text":"Pong."}
```

**Observed result after fix:** At current head `0d93c33d`, `sanitizeUserFacingText` removes the `Current message priority:` / `[Current message - respond to this]` / `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]` scaffolding blocks and standalone `(no output)` lines, returning only the genuine reply (`Pong.`, `Visible reply.`, `Before\nAfter`); it leaves both the inline-mention case (`The literal (no output) text is relevant.`) and the ordinary prose `Current message priority: high means this ticket is urgent, so I bumped it.` unchanged — the priority-marker line only triggers stripping inside a real scaffold block. `normalizeReplyPayload` suppresses a bare `(no output)` text-only payload (skip reason `empty-tool-output`) but preserves and still delivers payloads carrying media or channelData (clearing only the placeholder text to `""`), and reduces an echoed-scaffolding reply to the genuine text (`Pong.`).

**What was not tested:** A full end-to-end delivery through a live messaging channel (for example a real Telegram bot) was not exercised; verification runs the production `sanitizeUserFacingText` and `normalizeReplyPayload` functions directly against the issue inputs at current head `0d93c33d`.

target.title: [codex] Further optimize Telegram media and reply context flow
target.body:
## Summary

- add scoped indexes to the Telegram message cache so `recentBefore` and `around` avoid scanning and sorting the full cache
- cache successful Telegram media resolutions by file identity and relevant runtime options
- revalidate cached media paths before reuse so TTL cleanup cannot leave stale attachments behind
- add regression coverage for thread-scoped chat windows, media cache hits, and stale media cache eviction
- bump `@openclaw/fs-safe` to 0.2.1 so focused Telegram tests can install and resolve `@openclaw/fs-safe/config` cleanly

## Context

This builds on earlier Telegram-route performance work by `obviyus`, further optimizing the same flow by tightening reply-context lookups and avoiding repeated Telegram media hydration.

## Why

Telegram reply-context building can repeatedly query recent chat windows and hydrate the same reply media. This PR keeps those hot paths bounded while preserving the existing chat-wide and thread-specific behavior.

The media cache also checks the saved file still exists before returning a cached path, because long-running gateways can remove saved media through TTL cleanup.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram reply-context lookups stay thread-scoped or chat-wide as requested, repeated Telegram media hydration reuses the cached saved path, and a deleted saved media file is evicted and re-downloaded instead of returning a stale path.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway from this PR head (`b8ad83191132ea2cdb1c9a40312ded58f58444ba`) on macOS, running `OpenClaw 2026.5.10-beta.1` with the Telegram plugin loaded from the PR checkout. Telegram ingress used the real Bot API webhook for `@acecoderbot` through a temporary Cloudflare tunnel. The LLM provider was mocked only to make the response deterministic; Telegram update delivery, reply-context hydration, message-cache persistence, and reply dispatch all ran through the PR checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm openclaw --log-level debug gateway run --port 19043 --auth token --token [redacted] --bind loopback --verbose --ws-log compact` from the PR checkout with an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, a real Telegram Bot API webhook, and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible provider. Sent `PR80243 live seed OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_1` to `@acecoderbot`, then sent a real Telegram reply to that DM with `PR80243 live reply-context OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_2`. Also ran a local media/cache proof through `node --import tsx` using a real temporary state directory and local trusted Bot API file path.
- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live runtime logs and persisted cache/model-input excerpts from the actual PR gateway run:

```text

getFile_calls_after_stale_path_eviction=2
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Telegram reply reached OpenClaw with `reply_to_message`, OpenClaw logged `telegram reply-context` for `replyToId=10034`, the inbound prompt included `has_reply_context: true`, the nearby reply target window included both the original DM and the reply, and both Telegram messages completed through the PR gateway. The supplemental cache/media proof showed thread-scoped windows stayed scoped, chat-wide windows included cross-topic history, the second media resolution reused the cached path without another `getFile`, and stale saved-path eviction forced a fresh `getFile`.
- **What was not tested:** No additional gaps for the live Telegram reply-context behavior. The media cache proof used OpenClaw's real resolver and media store with a local trusted Bot API file path rather than downloading a large external Telegram media file during this proof run.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_HELD=1 pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.resolve-media-retry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`

target.title: fix(test): harden script probe bounds
target.body:
Summary:
- harden the kitchen-sink RPC POSIX process sampler so direct gateway roots are sampled without overriding the existing gateway-title child fallback
- reject unsafe decimal `content-length` values before reading kitchen-sink RPC probe bodies
- reject unsafe avatar probe `content-length` values before reading Clawtributors avatar bodies

Verification:
- `node --check scripts/e2e/kitchen-sink-rpc-walk.mjs`
- `node --check --experimental-strip-types scripts/update-clawtributors.ts`
- focused direct Node repros for the POSIX root sampler, gateway-title fallback, and unsafe kitchen-sink `content-length`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean)
- Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01kvggv467b8jgnemzyd97b79y`: fetched `qa-sre-rpc-kitchen-sink-20260619` in the full checkout, then ran `pnpm check:changed` with `lanes=tooling` successfully

Known proof gap:
- native Crabbox macOS run is blocked by broker auth/config: `provider=aws requires a configured Crabbox broker for OpenClaw proof`.
target.author: vincentkoc

target.title: fix #17447: [Bug]: Security audit: allow suppressing acknowledged findings + fix false positives
target.body:
## Summary

- Partially addresses #17447 by removing the false positive hook audit warning when request-supplied session keys are constrained by `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes`.
- Keeps the warning when `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey=true` is enabled without prefixes, where callers can still choose arbitrary session key shapes.
- Adds a focused regression test for the prefixed request-session-key audit configuration.
- Aligns the CLI security guide and audit-check catalog with the narrowed no-prefix warning semantics.
- Why it matters / User impact: Operators can enable request-scoped hook session keys with an explicit prefix allowlist without the security audit or docs claiming an arbitrary-session-key risk that the configured prefix boundary already prevents.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root cause for issue #17447, not a fan-out or merged-PR follow-up.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: The `status: waiting on author` label came from the ClawSweeper docs mismatch finding; this update addresses that author action by aligning the docs and will let the bot/status refresh after push.
- RF-002/RF-003/RF-004: Updated `docs/cli/security.md` and `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` so `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` is documented as the no-prefix/arbitrary-session-key-shape warning.
- RF-005: Re-ran `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` after the docs repair.
- RF-006: Re-ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts` after the docs repair.

## Root Cause

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for a security audit predicate false positive.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the diff is limited to the hook audit predicate, focused regression coverage, and matching operator-facing audit docs, and the source CLI audit path was exercised with isolated home, config, and state.
- Root cause: `collectHooksHardeningFindings` already normalized `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes`, and the missing-prefix finding used that normalized contract, but the older `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` branch ignored the normalized prefix invariant and fired solely on `hooks.allowRequestSessionKey=true`, causing prefixed configurations to be reported as arbitrary session-key override risk.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The fix changes the source audit invariant before findings are emitted: `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` now uses the same `allowRequestSessionKey && allowedPrefixes.length === 0` condition as the missing-prefix finding, so only configurations that permit arbitrary request session-key shapes produce the warning instead of masking the symptom after report generation.

## Scope

- What did NOT change: This PR does not change request routing, hook authorization, `security.audit.suppressions`, the small-model heuristic, reverse-proxy trusted-header findings, config defaults, migrations, or the public config schema. The related open PR / same-contract scan found no competing public-config PR before this PR was created, and the current update only addresses the existing ClawSweeper docs finding on this PR.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The source-of-truth is the hook hardening audit collector that turns normalized config into security findings. This patch changes that collector predicate directly, not a report post-processor or display mirror, and it preserves backward compatibility/default behavior because unprefixed request session-key overrides continue to emit both warnings.
- Patch quality notes: Patch-quality warnings considered: public-config surface, security-boundary, and session-state. The change is not symptom masking; it narrows one finding to the same source invariant already used by the stricter missing-prefix finding, with focused regression coverage and matching operator-facing docs.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw security audit --json` no longer emits `hooks.request_session_key_enabled` or `hooks.request_session_key_prefixes_missing` for a hooks config that enables request session keys but constrains them with `hooks.allowedSessionKeyPrefixes`.
- Real environment tested: Linux source checkout, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under the PR evidence directory.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
git -C /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95028 diff --check origin/main...HEAD

pnpm --dir /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95028 exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts

HOME=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-95028-evidence/audit-home \
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-95028-evidence/audit-home/.openclaw \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-95028-evidence/hooks-prefix-audit-config.json \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-95028-evidence/audit-state \
pnpm --dir /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95028 exec node --import tsx -e 'import { runCli } from "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95028/src/cli/run-main.ts"; await runCli(["node","openclaw","security","audit","--json"]);' \
> /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-95028-evidence/hooks-prefix-source-cli-audit-output-after-docs.json

node -e 'const fs=require("fs"); const report=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync("/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-95028-evidence/hooks-prefix-source-cli-audit-output-after-docs.json","utf8")); const ids=new Set((report.findings||[]).map(f=>f.checkId)); for (const id of ["hooks.request_session_key_enabled","hooks.request_session_key_prefixes_missing"]) { if (ids.has(id)) { console.error(`unexpected finding: ${id}`); process.exit(1); } } console.log(`existing-pr isolated source cli audit proof after docs ok: ${report.findings.length} findings, hook sessionKey findings absent`);'
```
- Evidence after fix:
```text
existing-pr isolated source cli audit proof after docs ok: 8 findings, hook sessionKey findings absent
```
- Observed result after fix: The actual source CLI `security audit --json` path completed and the parsed JSON report did not include either hook session-key finding for the prefixed configuration. The targeted regression test also passed with the documented `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` invocation.
- What was not tested: Built-package `openclaw.mjs` was not exercised because this source worktree does not include `dist/entry.mjs`; the source CLI command path was exercised instead. I did not test or change the other #17447 topics: general audit suppressions, small-model sandboxing heuristics, or reverse-proxy trusted-header warnings.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: The regression test constructs a hooks config with `allowRequestSessionKey: true` and `allowedSessionKeyPrefixes: ["hook:"]`, then verifies that both hook session-key findings are absent while the existing unprefixed warning coverage remains intact.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The test locks the audit collector behavior at the same source-of-truth predicate changed by this patch, while the source CLI proof separately exercises the real `security audit --json` command path.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: merge risk: security-boundary, session-state, public_config.
- Risk explanation: The touched code reports security audit findings and mentions session keys, so the risk is reviewer concern about weakening a security boundary or changing public config semantics. The patch does not relax hook authorization or routing; it only stops claiming arbitrary session-key shape risk when a configured prefix allowlist already constrains the accepted key space.
- Why acceptable: The compatibility/default behavior is unchanged for unprefixed configurations, which continue to emit both warnings. Prefixed configurations now align both findings and the docs to the same normalized-prefix contract, and the out of scope areas are explicitly left unchanged.

## Additional checks

```text
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
passed with no output

pnpm --dir /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-95028 exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       14 passed (14)
```

target.title: feat(feeds): install approved feed entries
target.body:
## Summary

Builds on the read-only feeds discovery PR by allowing an operator to explicitly install one selected feed entry, then optionally require approved feed metadata for feed-backed installs.

This PR keeps the install path explicit:

- Adds `openclaw feeds install <id>` for selected feed entries.
- Verifies pinned feed integrity before using feed install metadata.
- Resolves one feed entry and hands off to the existing OpenClaw skill/plugin install commands.
- Adds `installPolicy` with `off`, `warn`, and `enforce` modes.
- Makes `mode: "enforce"` and `requireApproval: true` default to approved-only behavior instead of silently no-oping.
- Adds schema and Doctor validation for the new install policy config.

## Example syntax

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "feeds": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "installPolicy": {
            "mode": "enforce",
            "requireApproval": true
          },
          "sources": [
            {
              "id": "company-approved",
              "url": "file:///opt/openclaw/feeds/company.json"
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Commands

```bash
openclaw feeds install calendar-helper --source company-approved --type plugin --dry-run
openclaw feeds install calendar-helper --source company-approved --type plugin
openclaw doctor --lint
```

## Notes

This PR does not auto-install, background-watch, or intercept non-feed install commands. It only constrains explicit feed-backed installs through the new `feeds install` path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Explicit feed-backed installs now resolve a selected feed entry, honor feed `installPolicy`, dry-run the delegated install command, install approved entries through the existing OpenClaw installer, and block unapproved entries when enforcement is enabled.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-87825` at `75abd34788ff68c7f62164e2f391d70e23478436`, source CLI via `pnpm openclaw`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `HOME`. The feed was a local `file://` feed fixture with one approved local skill entry and one unapproved local skill entry; the approved install used the real `openclaw skills install <local path>` path and wrote only into a temporary agent workspace.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
3. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md && git diff --check`
4. `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
5. Source CLI proof with a temporary config/feed/skill fixture: `pnpm openclaw feeds install local-proof-skill --source proof --type skill --dry-run`, `pnpm openclaw feeds install local-proof-skill --source proof --type skill --force`, and `pnpm openclaw feeds install blocked-proof-skill --source proof --type skill`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Vitest: 2 Vitest shards passed; 35 focused tests passed.
oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
tsgo: pnpm tsgo:extensions completed successfully.

CASE dry-run exit=0
openclaw skills install /tmp/openclaw-feeds-87825-proof.oKOgMz/source-skill

CASE approved-install exit=0
Installing to /tmp/openclaw-feeds-87825-proof.oKOgMz/workspace/skills/pr-proof-skill...
Installed pr-proof-skill from path -> /tmp/openclaw-feeds-87825-proof.oKOgMz/workspace/skills/pr-proof-skill

CASE enforcement-block exit=2
Feed entry 'blocked-proof-skill' is not approved by feed metadata. Set approval.status to approved or update feeds installPolicy.
[ELIFECYCLE] Command failed with exit code 2.

INSTALLED_SKILL_EXISTS=yes
---
name: pr-proof-skill
description: Feed install proof fixture.
---
# PR Proof Skill
```

Observed result after fix:
The dry-run printed the delegated existing installer command without installing, the approved feed entry installed through the real source CLI into the temporary workspace, and the unapproved entry was rejected before installer dispatch when `installPolicy` enforced approval.

What was not tested:
No live ClawHub network install was performed; the approved install proof intentionally used a local skill path fixture to avoid external mutation while still exercising the real OpenClaw source CLI and installer dispatch.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

target.title: fix(gateway): bind MCP loopback scope to tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Bind MCP loopback session/account/channel context to server-issued scoped bearer tokens.
- Stop emitting mutable loopback scope headers in bundled MCP config and CLI env.
- Add regressions proving spoofed scope/owner headers do not change loopback tool context.

## Motivation
The previous April owner-context fix made `senderIsOwner` derive from owner/non-owner bearer tokens, but the same loopback endpoint still accepted `x-session-key`, `x-openclaw-account-id`, `x-openclaw-message-channel`, and `x-openclaw-inbound-event-kind` from every request. A process with a valid loopback bearer could therefore keep non-owner status but choose a different session/account/channel/event policy context. Those fields feed `resolveGatewayScopedTools(...)`, group/session policy, room-event message-tool behavior, and hook context.

This PR closes that adjacent trust-boundary gap by issuing per-run scoped loopback tokens and resolving the full MCP loopback context from the server-side token registry instead of request headers.

## Rebase notes (current revision)
Rebased onto current `main`, which had grown five more header-driven context fields on the same loopback surface (`x-openclaw-current-channel-id`, `x-openclaw-current-thread-ts`, `x-openclaw-current-message-id`, `x-openclaw-current-inbound-audio`, `x-openclaw-source-reply-delivery-mode`). Those are the same spoofable surface, so the scoped token now carries the full context: `sessionKey`, `messageProvider`, `currentChannelId`, `currentThreadTs`, `currentMessageId`, `currentInboundAudio`, `accountId`, `inboundEventKind`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `senderIsOwner`. The bundled MCP server config now sends only the `Authorization` bearer header; all `x-*` scope headers and their `OPENCLAW_MCP_*` env plumbing are gone. The live-probe helper keeps main's request timeout + bounded body read and issues/revokes a scoped owner token per call.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: MCP loopback requests with spoofed scope and owner headers should still resolve tool context from the server-issued scoped bearer token.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the actual `src/gateway/mcp-http.ts` loopback server with `OPENCLAW_CLI_BACKEND_LOG_OUTPUT=1` and `node --import tsx --input-type=module`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the real MCP loopback server, issued a scoped non-owner bearer token for `agent:main:matrix:dm:issued` with `inboundEventKind=user_request`, then called `tools/list` while sending spoofed headers for `agent:main:telegram:group:spoofed`, `accountId=spoofed-account`, `messageProvider=telegram`, `inboundEventKind=room_event`, and `x-openclaw-sender-is-owner=true`.

Evidence after fix:
```text
[mcp-loopback] request {"batchSize":1,"methods":["tools/list"],"sessionKey":"agent:main:matrix:dm:issued","inboundEventKind":"user_request","senderIsOwner":false,"toolCount":20,"cronVisible":false}
[mcp-loopback] response {"method":"tools/list","isError":false}
```

Observed result after fix: The request succeeded, but the runtime log shows the server ignored the spoofed scope/owner headers and resolved `sessionKey=agent:main:matrix:dm:issued`, `inboundEventKind=user_request`, and `senderIsOwner=false` from the scoped token. The rebased revision additionally covers the new context fields with `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts` regressions ("does not let spoofed scope headers change resolved loopback context", "ignores spoofed owner and scope headers when the bearer token is non-owner scoped", and the scoped-token context-propagation test asserting all ten fields reach `resolveGatewayScopedTools`).

What was not tested: No known gaps.

## Test plan (run on the rebased head)
- pnpm test src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts (146 passed)
- pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts (passed)
- pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test
- node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files> (clean)
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files> (clean)

target.title: fix(telegram): materialize preview drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #95004.

This updates Telegram preview streaming in two places:

- Short first previews that stay below `minInitialChars` now materialize after two throttle windows, using the latest pending text and cancelling cleanly on discard, clear, reset, or threshold send.
- Answer-only partials in progress mode now create the existing label-only progress preview through the progress draft compositor, while still keeping answer text out of the draft stream and preserving final durable delivery.

## Verification

- Red reproduction before the fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts -t "minInitialChars"` failed on the new delayed-materialization expectation with zero `sendMessage` calls.
- Passed after the fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts -t "minInitialChars"`.
- Passed after the fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts -t "answer-only progress-mode"`.
- Passed after the fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts -t "generic activity"`.
- Passed full touched unit files: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.
- Passed: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.ts`.
- Passed: `git diff --check`.
- Passed: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`.
- Passed: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`.
- Passed: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`.
- Passed: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`.
- Post-rebase lightweight proof passed in the linked worktree: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

Autoreview was attempted once per the draft-PR guardrail, but the helper failed before reviewing because the local Codex config uses `service_tier = priority`, which this Codex binary rejects. No autoreview findings were produced.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram preview streaming now materializes short initial drafts below `minInitialChars` after a bounded delay, and progress-mode answer-only streams now show a progress preview instead of suppressing all draft activity until final delivery.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with focused Telegram/channel unit coverage and type/format checks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The focused and full touched-file commands listed in `Verification` were run after the patch; the branch was also rebased onto current `origin/main` and checked with `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`.

Evidence after fix: The delayed short-preview test now observes `sendMessage`; the answer-only progress-mode test observes a label-only draft update, no answer text draft update, draft cleanup, and final durable delivery.

Observed result after fix: Focused tests, full touched unit tests, format check, diff check, and relevant tsgo lanes passed.
